tags: Part B. Your new colleague, Ms. Parker is known to have two children. If you see her walki
problems.promptEn: Part B. Your new colleague, Ms. Parker is known to have two children. If you see her walking with one of her children and that child is a boy, what is the probability that both children are boys?
problems.promptZh: B 部分。众所周知，你的新同事帕克女士有两个孩子。如果你看到她和她的一个孩子一起散步，而那个孩子是男孩，那么两个孩子都是男孩的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: the other child is equally likely to be a boy or a girl (independent of the boy you've seen), so the probability that both children are boys is 1/2. Notice the subtle difference between part A and part B. In part A, the problem essentially asks given there is at least one boy in two children, what is the conditional probability that both children are boys. Part B asks that given one child is a boy, what is the conditional probability that the other child is also a boy. For both parts, we need to assume that each child is equal likely to be a boy or a girl.
problems.explanationEn: the other child is equally likely to be a boy or a girl (independent of the boy you've seen), so the probability that both children are boys is 1/2. Notice the subtle difference between part A and part B. In part A, the problem essentially asks given there is at least one boy in two children, what is the conditional probability that both children are boys. Part B asks that given one child is a boy, what is the conditional probability that the other child is also a boy. For both parts, we need to assume that each child is equal likely to be a boy or a girl.
problems.explanationZh: 另一个孩子是男孩或女孩的可能性相同（与您见过的男孩无关），因此两个孩子都是男孩的概率是 1/2。请注意 A 部分和 B 部分之间的细微差别。在 A 部分中，问题本质上是问，假设两个孩子中至少有一个是男孩，那么两个孩子都是男孩的条件概率是多少。 B 部分询问，给定一个孩子是男孩，另一个孩子也是男孩的条件概率是多少。对于这两部分，我们需要假设每个孩子成为男孩或女孩的可能性相同。

problems.promptEn: In a primitive society, every couple prefers to have a baby girl. There is a 50% chance that each child they have is a girl, and the genders of their children are mutually independent. If each couple insists on having more children until they get a girl and once they have a girl they will stop having more children, what will eventually happen to the fraction of girls in this society?
problems.promptZh: 在原始社会，每对夫妇都希望生一个女儿。他们每个孩子都有50%的几率是女孩，而且孩子的性别是相互独立的。如果每对夫妇都坚持要多生，直到生女孩，一旦生了女孩就不再生孩子，那么这个社会中女孩的比例最终会怎样呢？
problems.explanation: It was surprising that many interviewees-include many who studied probability-have the misconception that there will be more girls. Do not let the word "prefer" and a wrong intuition misguide you. The fraction of baby girls are driven by nature, or at least the X and Y chromosomes, not by the couples' preference. You only need to look at the key information: 50% and independence. Every new-born child has equal probability of being a boy or a girl regardless of the gender of any other children. So the fraction of girls born is always 50% and the fractions of girls in the society will stay stable at 50%.
problems.explanationEn: It was surprising that many interviewees-include many who studied probability-have the misconception that there will be more girls. Do not let the word "prefer" and a wrong intuition misguide you. The fraction of baby girls are driven by nature, or at least the X and Y chromosomes, not by the couples' preference. You only need to look at the key information: 50% and independence. Every new-born child has equal probability of being a boy or a girl regardless of the gender of any other children. So the fraction of girls born is always 50% and the fractions of girls in the society will stay stable at 50%.
problems.explanationZh: 令人惊讶的是，许多受访者（包括许多研究概率论的人）错误地认为将会有更多的女孩。不要让“更喜欢”这个词和错误的直觉误导您。女婴的比例是由天性决定的，或者至少是由 X 和 Y 染色体决定的，而不是由夫妇的偏好决定的。你只需要看关键信息：50%和独立性。无论其他孩子的性别如何，每个新生儿都有相同的概率成为男孩或女孩。因此，出生的女孩的比例始终为50%，社会中女孩的比例也将稳定在50%。

problems.promptEn: You are given 1000 coins. Among them, 1 coin has heads on both sides. The other 999 coins are fair coins. You randomly choose a coin and toss it 10 times. Each time, the coin turns up heads. What is the probability that the coin you choose is the unfair one?
problems.promptZh: 您将获得 1000 个硬币。其中，1枚硬币两面都是头像。其余999枚均为公平币。您随机选择一枚硬币并抛掷 10 次。每次，硬币都会正面朝上。您选择的硬币是不公平硬币的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: This is a classic conditional probability question that uses Bayes' theorem. Let A be the event that the chosen coin is the unfair one, then A" is the event that the chosen coin is a fair one. Let B be the event that all ten tosses tum up heads. Apply Bayes' theorem we have P( A I B) = P( B I A)P( A) = P( B I A)P( A) P(B) P(B I A)P(A) + P(B I A")P(A") The priors are P( A) = 1/1000 and P( A") = 99911000. If the coin is unfair, it always turns up heads, so P(B I A)= 1. If the coin is fair, each time it has 1/2 probability turning up heads. So P(B I A")= (1I2)10 = 111024. Plug in all the available information and we have the answer: P(AIB)- P(BIA)P(A) = 1/lOOOxl P(B I A)P(A) + P(B I A' )P(A') 1/1000x1+999/1000x1I1024
problems.explanationEn: This is a classic conditional probability question that uses Bayes' theorem. Let A be the event that the chosen coin is the unfair one, then A" is the event that the chosen coin is a fair one. Let B be the event that all ten tosses tum up heads. Apply Bayes' theorem we have P( A I B) = P( B I A)P( A) = P( B I A)P( A) P(B) P(B I A)P(A) + P(B I A")P(A") The priors are P( A) = 1/1000 and P( A") = 99911000. If the coin is unfair, it always turns up heads, so P(B I A)= 1. If the coin is fair, each time it has 1/2 probability turning up heads. So P(B I A")= (1I2)10 = 111024. Plug in all the available information and we have the answer: P(AIB)- P(BIA)P(A) = 1/lOOOxl P(B I A)P(A) + P(B I A' )P(A') 1/1000x1+999/1000x1I1024
problems.explanationZh: 这是一个使用贝叶斯定理的经典条件概率问题。设 A 为所选硬币为不公平硬币的事件，则 A" 为所选硬币为公平硬币的事件。设 B 为所有 10 次抛掷都正面朝上的事件。应用贝叶斯定理，我们有 P( A I B) = P( B I A)P( A) = P( B I A)P( A) P(B) P(B I A)P(A) + P(B I A")P(A") 先验为 P( A) = 1/1000 且 P( A") = 99911000。如果硬币是不公平的，它总是正面朝上，所以 P(B I A)= 1。如果硬币是公平的，每次正面朝上的概率是 1/2。所以 P(B I A")= (1I2)10 = 111024。代入所有可用信息，我们得到答案： P(AIB)- P(BIA)P(A) = 1/lOOOxl P(B I A)P(A) + P(B I A' )P(A') 1/1000x1+999/1000x1I1024

problems.titleEn: Fair probability from an unfair coin
problems.titleZh: 不公平硬币的公平概率
tags: Fair probability from an unfair coin
problems.promptEn: If you have an unfair coin, which may bias toward either heads or tails at an unknown probability, can you generate even odds using this coin?
problems.promptZh: 如果你有一枚不公平的硬币，它可能会以未知的概率偏向正面或反面，你能使用这枚硬币产生偶数赔率吗？
problems.explanation: Unlike fair coins, we clearly can not generate even odds with one toss using an unfair coin. How about using 2 tosses? Let PH be the probability the coin will yield head, and Pr be the probability the coin will yield tails ( pH + Pr = 1 ). Consider two independent tosses. We have four possible outcomes HH, HT, TH and TT with probabilities P(HH) = PHPH, P(HT) = PHPr> P(TH) =Pr PH' and P(TT) =Pr Pr. So we have P(HT) =P(TH). By assigning HT to winning and TH to losing, we can generate even odds.
problems.explanationEn: Unlike fair coins, we clearly can not generate even odds with one toss using an unfair coin. How about using 2 tosses? Let PH be the probability the coin will yield head, and Pr be the probability the coin will yield tails ( pH + Pr = 1 ). Consider two independent tosses. We have four possible outcomes HH, HT, TH and TT with probabilities P(HH) = PHPH, P(HT) = PHPr> P(TH) =Pr PH' and P(TT) =Pr Pr. So we have P(HT) =P(TH). By assigning HT to winning and TH to losing, we can generate even odds.
problems.explanationZh: 与公平的硬币不同，我们显然无法使用不公平的硬币进行一次抛掷来产生均匀的赔率。使用 2 次抛掷怎么样？令 PH 为硬币出现正面的概率，Pr 为硬币出现反面的概率 ( pH + Pr = 1 )。考虑两次独立的抛掷。我们有四种可能的结果 HH、HT、TH 和 TT，概率为 P(HH) = PHPH、P(HT) = PHPr> P(TH) =Pr PH' 和 P(TT) =Pr Pr。所以我们有 P(HT) =P(TH)。通过将 HT 分配给获胜，将 TH 分配给失败，我们可以生成偶数赔率。

problems.promptEn: Jason throws two darts at a dartboard, aiming for the center. The second dart lands farther from the center than the first. If Jason throws a third dart aiming for the center, what is the probability that the third throw is farther from the center than the first? Assume Jason's skillfulness is constant.
problems.promptZh: 杰森向飞镖靶投掷两支飞镖，瞄准中心。第二个飞镖比第一个飞镖落在距离中心更远的地方。如果杰森朝中心投掷第三支飞镖，则第三次投掷比第一次投掷的飞镖距离中心更远的概率是多少？假设杰森的技巧是恒定的。
problems.explanation:
A standard answer directly applies conditional probability by enumerating all possible outcomes. If we rank the three darts' results from best ($A$) to worst ($C$), there are six equally likely orders. The information that the second throw is worse than the first eliminates the orders in which the second throw is better than the first. Among the remaining three orders, the third throw is worse than the first in two of them. Therefore the desired probability is $2/3$.

There is also a cleaner symmetry argument. In a generalized version, suppose Jason throws $n$ darts, with the first throw the best among those $n$ throws. If he then throws dart $n+1$, the event that dart $n+1$ is the best among all $n+1$ throws is independent of the order of the first $n$ throws. That event has probability $1/(n+1)$, so the probability that dart $n+1$ is not the best is $n/(n+1)$. For the original case $n=2$, this gives $2/3$.
problems.explanationEn:
A standard answer directly applies conditional probability by enumerating all possible outcomes. If we rank the three darts' results from best ($A$) to worst ($C$), there are six equally likely orders. The information that the second throw is worse than the first eliminates the orders in which the second throw is better than the first. Among the remaining three orders, the third throw is worse than the first in two of them. Therefore the desired probability is $2/3$.

There is also a cleaner symmetry argument. In a generalized version, suppose Jason throws $n$ darts, with the first throw the best among those $n$ throws. If he then throws dart $n+1$, the event that dart $n+1$ is the best among all $n+1$ throws is independent of the order of the first $n$ throws. That event has probability $1/(n+1)$, so the probability that dart $n+1$ is not the best is $n/(n+1)$. For the original case $n=2$, this gives $2/3$.
problems.explanationZh:
标准答案可以直接枚举条件概率。把三支飞镖的结果从最好记为 $A$ 到最差记为 $C$，共有六种等可能顺序。已知第二次投掷比第一次更差，因此排除所有第二次优于第一次的顺序。在剩下的三种顺序中，第三次投掷比第一次更差的情况有两种。所以所求概率为 $2/3$。

也可以用更简洁的对称性理解。推广来看，假设 Jason 已投掷 $n$ 支飞镖，且第一支是这 $n$ 支中最好的。如果再投第 $n+1$ 支，那么“第 $n+1$ 支是全部 $n+1$ 支中最好的一支”这一事件，与前 $n$ 支飞镖的内部顺序无关。该事件概率为 $1/(n+1)$，因此第 $n+1$ 支不是最好的一支的概率为 $n/(n+1)$。原题对应 $n=2$，所以答案为 $2/3$。

problems.promptEn: At a movie theater, a whimsical manager announces that she will give a free ticket to the first person in line whose birthday is the same as someone who has already bought a ticket. You are given the opportunity to choose any position in line. Assuming that you don't know anyone else's birthday and all birthdays are distributed randomly throughout the year (assuming 365 dals in a year), what position in line gives you the largest chance of getting the free ticket?
problems.promptZh: 在一家电影院，一位经理宣布：她会把一张免费票送给队伍中第一个生日与已经买过票的人相同的人。你可以选择自己在队伍中的任意位置。假设你不知道其他人的生日，并且生日在一年 365 天中均匀随机分布，那么你站在第几位最有可能拿到免费票？
problems.answer: 20th
problems.answerEn: 20th
problems.answerZh: 第 20 位
problems.explanation:
Suppose you choose position $n$. You get the ticket exactly when the first $n-1$ birthdays are all distinct and your birthday matches one of them. Thus
$$p(n)=\frac{365\cdot364\cdots(365-n+2)}{365^{n-1}}\cdot\frac{n-1}{365}.$$
Compare consecutive probabilities:
$$\frac{p(n+1)}{p(n)}=\frac{365-(n-1)}{365}\cdot\frac{n}{n-1}=\frac{n(366-n)}{365(n-1)}.$$
This ratio is greater than $1$ through $n=19$ and less than $1$ at $n=20$. Hence $p(n)$ is maximized at $n=20$.
problems.explanationEn:
Suppose you choose position $n$. You get the ticket exactly when the first $n-1$ birthdays are all distinct and your birthday matches one of them. Thus
$$p(n)=\frac{365\cdot364\cdots(365-n+2)}{365^{n-1}}\cdot\frac{n-1}{365}.$$
Compare consecutive probabilities:
$$\frac{p(n+1)}{p(n)}=\frac{365-(n-1)}{365}\cdot\frac{n}{n-1}=\frac{n(366-n)}{365(n-1)}.$$
This ratio is greater than $1$ through $n=19$ and less than $1$ at $n=20$. Hence $p(n)$ is maximized at $n=20$.
problems.explanationZh:
假设你选择第 $n$ 位。你拿到票，当且仅当前 $n-1$ 个人生日互不相同，并且你的生日与其中某个人相同。因此
$$p(n)=\frac{365\cdot364\cdots(365-n+2)}{365^{n-1}}\cdot\frac{n-1}{365}.$$
比较相邻概率：
$$\frac{p(n+1)}{p(n)}=\frac{365-(n-1)}{365}\cdot\frac{n}{n-1}=\frac{n(366-n)}{365(n-1)}.$$
这个比值到 $n=19$ 时仍大于 $1$，到 $n=20$ 时小于 $1$。因此 $p(n)$ 在 $n=20$ 处最大。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Birthday-line probability ratios were garbled; answer fields were empty.

problems.promptEn: We throw 3 dice one by one. What is the probability that we obtain 3 points in strictly increasing order?
problems.promptZh: 依次掷 3 个骰子。得到的 3 个点数严格递增的概率是多少？
problems.explanation:
There are $6^3=216$ equally likely ordered outcomes. A strictly increasing triple is determined by choosing any 3 distinct faces from the 6 faces and arranging them in increasing order. Thus the number of favorable outcomes is
$$\binom{6}{3}=20.$$
Therefore the probability is
$$\frac{20}{216}=\frac{5}{54}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
There are $6^3=216$ equally likely ordered outcomes. A strictly increasing triple is determined by choosing any 3 distinct faces from the 6 faces and arranging them in increasing order. Thus the number of favorable outcomes is
$$\binom{6}{3}=20.$$
Therefore the probability is
$$\frac{20}{216}=\frac{5}{54}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
共有 $6^3=216$ 个等可能的有序结果。一个严格递增的三元组，只需从 6 个点数中选出 3 个不同点数，并按递增顺序排列即可。因此有利结果数为
$$\binom{6}{3}=20.$$
概率为
$$\frac{20}{216}=\frac{5}{54}.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Final probability was truncated.

problems.promptEn: Monty Hall problem is a probability puzzle based on an old American show Let's Make a Deal. The problem is named after the show's host. Suppose you're on the show now, and you're given the choice of 3 doors. Behind one door is a car; behind the other two, goats. You don't know ahead of time what is behind each of the doors. You pick one of the doors and announce it. As soon as you pick the door, Monty opens one of the other two doors that he knows has a goat behind it. Then he gives you the option to either keep your original choice or switch to the third door. Should you switch? What is the probability of winning a car if you switch?
problems.promptZh: 蒙蒂·霍尔问题是一个基于美国老节目《Let's Make a Deal》的概率难题。该问题以节目主持人的名字命名。假设您现在正在节目中，并且您可以选择 3 扇门。一扇门后面是一辆汽车；在另外两个后面，是山羊。您提前不知道每扇门后面是什么。你选择其中一扇门并宣布。一旦你选择了门，蒙蒂就会打开另外两扇门中的一扇，他知道门后面有一只山羊。然后他让你选择保留原来的选择或切换到第三扇门。你应该切换吗？如果您更换，赢得汽车的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: If you don't switch, whether you win or not is independent of Monty's action of showing you a goat, so your probability of winning is 1/3. What if you switch? Many would argue that since there are only two doors left after Monty shows a door with goat, the probability of winning is 1/2. But is this argument correct? If you look at the problem from a different perspective, the answer becomes clear. Using a switching strategy, you win the car if and only if you originally pick a door with a goat, which has a probability of 2/3 (You pick a door with a goat, Monty shows a door with another goat, so the one you switch to must have a car behind it). If you originally picked the door with the car, which has a probability of 113, you will lose by switching. So your probability of winning by switching is actually 2/3.
problems.explanationEn: If you don't switch, whether you win or not is independent of Monty's action of showing you a goat, so your probability of winning is 1/3. What if you switch? Many would argue that since there are only two doors left after Monty shows a door with goat, the probability of winning is 1/2. But is this argument correct? If you look at the problem from a different perspective, the answer becomes clear. Using a switching strategy, you win the car if and only if you originally pick a door with a goat, which has a probability of 2/3 (You pick a door with a goat, Monty shows a door with another goat, so the one you switch to must have a car behind it). If you originally picked the door with the car, which has a probability of 113, you will lose by switching. So your probability of winning by switching is actually 2/3.
problems.explanationZh: 如果你不切换，你是否获胜与 Monty 向你展示山羊的动作无关，所以你获胜的概率是 1/3。如果你换了怎么办？许多人会争辩说，由于蒙蒂展示了一扇有山羊的门后只剩下两扇门，所以获胜的概率是 1/2。但这个论点正确吗？如果你从不同的角度看问题，答案就很清楚了。使用切换策略，当且仅当您最初选择一扇有山羊的门时，您才能赢得汽车，概率为 2/3（您选择一扇有山羊的门，Monty 显示一扇有另一只山羊的门，因此您切换到的门后面必须有一辆车）。如果你最初选择了有车的门，概率为113，那么你会因为切换而输。所以你通过转换获胜的概率实际上是 2/3。

problems.promptEn: There is a one amoeba in a pond. After every minute the amoeba may die, stay the same, split into two or split into three with equal probability. All its offspring, if it has any, will behave the same (and independent of other amoebas). What is the probability the amoeba population will die out?
problems.promptZh: 池塘里有一只阿米巴原虫。每分钟之后，阿米巴原虫就会以相同的概率死亡、保持不变、分裂成两个或分裂成三个。它的所有后代（如果有的话）都会表现相同（并且独立于其他阿米巴原虫）。阿米巴原虫种群灭绝的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: This is just another standard conditional probability problem once you realize we need to derive the probability conditioned on what happens to the amoeba one minute later. Let P(E) be the probability that the amoeba population will die out and apply the law of total probability conditioned on what happens to the amoeba one minute later: For the original amoeba, as stated in the question, there are four possible mutually exclusive events each with probability 1/4. Let's denote F; as the event the amoeba dies; F2 as the event that it stays the same; F; as the event that it splits into two; F4 as the event that it splits into three. For event F;, P( E I F;) = I since no amoeba is left. P(E I F2 ) = P(E) since the state is the same as the beginning. For F;, there are two amoebas; either behaves the same as the original one. The total amoeba population will die only if both amoebas die out. Since they are independent, the probability that they both will die out is P(E) 2 • Similarly we have P(F4 ) = P(E)3. Plug in all the numbers, the equation becomes P(E)= 1/4xl+I/4xP(E)+1/4xP(E)2 +1/4xP(E)3 • Solve this equation with the restriction 0 < P(E) <I, and we will get P(E) = J2-I 0.414 (The other two roots of the equation are I and -J2 - 1 ).
problems.explanationEn: This is just another standard conditional probability problem once you realize we need to derive the probability conditioned on what happens to the amoeba one minute later. Let P(E) be the probability that the amoeba population will die out and apply the law of total probability conditioned on what happens to the amoeba one minute later: For the original amoeba, as stated in the question, there are four possible mutually exclusive events each with probability 1/4. Let's denote F; as the event the amoeba dies; F2 as the event that it stays the same; F; as the event that it splits into two; F4 as the event that it splits into three. For event F;, P( E I F;) = I since no amoeba is left. P(E I F2 ) = P(E) since the state is the same as the beginning. For F;, there are two amoebas; either behaves the same as the original one. The total amoeba population will die only if both amoebas die out. Since they are independent, the probability that they both will die out is P(E) 2 • Similarly we have P(F4 ) = P(E)3. Plug in all the numbers, the equation becomes P(E)= 1/4xl+I/4xP(E)+1/4xP(E)2 +1/4xP(E)3 • Solve this equation with the restriction 0 < P(E) <I, and we will get P(E) = J2-I 0.414 (The other two roots of the equation are I and -J2 - 1 ).
problems.explanationZh: 一旦您意识到我们需要根据变形虫一分钟后发生的情况得出条件概率，这只是另一个标准条件概率问题。设 P(E) 为变形虫种群灭绝的概率，并应用以变形虫一分钟后发生的情况为条件的总概率定律：对于原始变形虫，如问题中所述，有四种可能的互斥事件，每种事件的概率为 1/4。让我们用F来表示；当阿米巴原虫死亡时； F2 作为保持不变的事件； F；作为它分裂成两部分的事件； F4为将其一分为三的事件。对于事件 F;，P(E I F;) = I，因为没有阿米巴原虫留下。 P(E I F2 ) = P(E) 因为状态与开始时相同。对于F；，有两个变形虫；任一行为与原始行为相同。只有当两条阿米巴虫都灭绝时，阿米巴虫总数才会死亡。由于它们是独立的，因此它们都消亡的概率为 P(E) 2 • 类似地，我们有 P(F4 ) = P(E)3。代入所有数字，方程变为 P(E)= 1/4xl+I/4xP(E)+1/4xP(E)2 +1/4xP(E)3 • 在 0 < P(E) <I 的限制下求解该方程，我们将得到 P(E) = J2-I 0.414 （方程的另外两个根是 I 和 -J2 - 1 ）。

problems.promptEn: You are taking candies one by one from a jar that has 10 red candies, 20 blue candies, and 30 green candies in it. What is the probability that there is at least 1 blue candy and 1 green candy left in the jar when you have taken out all the red candies?
problems.promptZh: 一个罐子里有 10 颗红糖、20 颗蓝糖和 30 颗绿糖。你从罐子里一颗一颗不放回地取糖。当所有红糖都被取出时，罐子里至少还剩 1 颗蓝糖和 1 颗绿糖的概率是多少？
problems.explanation:
Think of the draw order as a random permutation of the candies. We need the last red candy to appear before the last blue candy and before the last green candy.

If the absolute last candy is green, which has probability $30/60$, then among the red and blue candies, the last one must be blue. That conditional probability is $20/(10+20)=20/30$.

If the absolute last candy is blue, which has probability $20/60$, then among the red and green candies, the last one must be green. That conditional probability is $30/(10+30)=30/40$.

Therefore
$$\frac{30}{60}\cdot\frac{20}{30}+\frac{20}{60}\cdot\frac{30}{40}=\frac{7}{12}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
Think of the draw order as a random permutation of the candies. We need the last red candy to appear before the last blue candy and before the last green candy.

If the absolute last candy is green, which has probability $30/60$, then among the red and blue candies, the last one must be blue. That conditional probability is $20/(10+20)=20/30$.

If the absolute last candy is blue, which has probability $20/60$, then among the red and green candies, the last one must be green. That conditional probability is $30/(10+30)=30/40$.

Therefore
$$\frac{30}{60}\cdot\frac{20}{30}+\frac{20}{60}\cdot\frac{30}{40}=\frac{7}{12}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
把取糖顺序看成糖果的一个随机排列。我们需要最后一颗红糖早于最后一颗蓝糖，也早于最后一颗绿糖。

如果绝对最后一颗糖是绿色，概率为 $30/60$，那么在红糖和蓝糖中，最后出现的必须是蓝糖。这个条件概率为 $20/(10+20)=20/30$。

如果绝对最后一颗糖是蓝色，概率为 $20/60$，那么在红糖和绿糖中，最后出现的必须是绿糖。这个条件概率为 $30/(10+30)=30/40$。

因此概率为
$$\frac{30}{60}\cdot\frac{20}{30}+\frac{20}{60}\cdot\frac{30}{40}=\frac{7}{12}.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Candy counts and final probability were OCR-corrupted.

problems.promptEn: Two players, A and B, alternatively toss a fair coin (A tosses the coin first, then B tosses the coin, then A, then B .. . ). The sequence of heads and tails is recorded. If there is a head followed by a tail (HT subsequence), the game ends and the person who tosses the tail wins. What is the probability that A wins the game?
problems.promptZh: 两个玩家，A 和 B，轮流抛一枚公平的硬币（A 先抛硬币，然后 B 抛硬币，然后是 A，然后是 B......）。记录头部和尾部的顺序。如果有一个头跟着一个尾（HT 子序列），则游戏结束，抛尾的人获胜。 A 赢得比赛的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: Let P(A) be the probability that A wins; then the probability that B wins is P( B) = 1- P( A). Let's condition P( A) on A's first toss, which has 1I2 probability of H (heads) and 1/ 2 probability of T (tails). P(A) = 1/2P(A I H) + l/2P(A IT) If A's first toss is T, then B essentially becomes the first to toss (An His required for the HT subsequence). So we have P( A IT) = P( B) = 1-P( A). If A's first toss ends in H, let's further condition on B's first toss. B has 1/2 probability of getting T, in that case A loses. For the I I 2 probability that B gets H, B essentially becomes the first one to toss an H. In that case, A has ( 1- P( A I H)) probability of winning. So P( A I H) = 1I2 x 0 + l I 2 ( 1 - P( A I H)) P( A I H) = 1I3 Combining all the available information, we have P(A) = 1/ 2x 113+ 1/ 2(1-P(A)) P(A) = 419. Sanity check: we can see that P(A) < 1I2, which is reasonable since A cannot win in his first toss, yet B has 1/4 probability to win in her first toss.
problems.explanationEn: Let P(A) be the probability that A wins; then the probability that B wins is P( B) = 1- P( A). Let's condition P( A) on A's first toss, which has 1I2 probability of H (heads) and 1/ 2 probability of T (tails). P(A) = 1/2P(A I H) + l/2P(A IT) If A's first toss is T, then B essentially becomes the first to toss (An His required for the HT subsequence). So we have P( A IT) = P( B) = 1-P( A). If A's first toss ends in H, let's further condition on B's first toss. B has 1/2 probability of getting T, in that case A loses. For the I I 2 probability that B gets H, B essentially becomes the first one to toss an H. In that case, A has ( 1- P( A I H)) probability of winning. So P( A I H) = 1I2 x 0 + l I 2 ( 1 - P( A I H)) P( A I H) = 1I3 Combining all the available information, we have P(A) = 1/ 2x 113+ 1/ 2(1-P(A)) P(A) = 419. Sanity check: we can see that P(A) < 1I2, which is reasonable since A cannot win in his first toss, yet B has 1/4 probability to win in her first toss.
problems.explanationZh: 令 P(A) 为 A 获胜的概率；那么 B 获胜的概率为 P( B) = 1- P( A)。让我们以 A 的第一次抛掷为条件 P( A)，其中 H（正面）的概率为 1I2，T（反面）的概率为 1/ 2。 P(A) = 1/2P(A I H) + l/2P(A IT) 如果 A 的第一次抛掷是 T，则 B 实质上成为第一个抛掷（HT 子序列需要 His）。所以我们有 P( A IT) = P( B) = 1-P( A)。如果 A 的第一次抛掷以 H 结束，让我们进一步以 B 的第一次抛掷为条件。 B 有 1/2 的概率得到 T，在这种情况下 A 就输了。对于 B 获得 H 的 I I 2 概率，B 本质上成为第一个抛出 H 的人。在这种情况下，A 获胜的概率为 ( 1- P( A I H))。所以 P( A I H) = 1I2 x 0 + l I 2 ( 1 - P( A I H)) P( A I H) = 1I3 结合所有可用信息，我们有 P(A) = 1/ 2x 113+ 1/ 2(1-P(A)) P(A) = 419。 健全性检查：我们可以看到 P(A) < 1I2，这是合理的，因为 A 无法获胜他的第一次抛掷，而 B 在第一次抛掷中获胜的概率为 1/4。

problems.promptEn: Let's play a traditional version of Russian roulette. A single bullet is put into a 6- chamber revolver. The barrel is randomly spun so that each chamber is equally likely to be under the hammer. Two players take turns to pull the trigger-with the gun unfortunately pointing at one's own head-without further spinning until the gun goes off and the person who gets killed loses. If you, one of the players, can choose to go first or second, how will you choose? And what is your probability of loss?
problems.promptZh: 让我们玩传统版的俄罗斯轮盘赌。一颗子弹被放入六膛左轮手枪中。枪管随机旋转，以便每个腔室受到锤子打击的可能性相同。两名玩家轮流扣动扳机——不幸的是，枪指着自己的头——没有进一步旋转，直到枪响，被杀的人就输了。如果你作为一名玩家可以选择第一或第二，你会如何选择？您损失的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: Many people have the wrong impression that the first person has higher probability ofloss. After all, the first player has a 116 chance of getting killed in the first round before the second player starts. Unfortunately, this is one of the few times that intuition is wrong. Once the barrel is spun, the position of the bullet is fixed. If you go first, you lose if and only if the bullet is in chamber 1, 3 and 5. So the probability that you lose is the same as the second player, 1/2. In that sense, whether to go first or second does not matter.
problems.explanationEn: Many people have the wrong impression that the first person has higher probability ofloss. After all, the first player has a 116 chance of getting killed in the first round before the second player starts. Unfortunately, this is one of the few times that intuition is wrong. Once the barrel is spun, the position of the bullet is fixed. If you go first, you lose if and only if the bullet is in chamber 1, 3 and 5. So the probability that you lose is the same as the second player, 1/2. In that sense, whether to go first or second does not matter.
problems.explanationZh: 很多人有一个错误的印象，认为第一个人损失的概率更高。毕竟，在第二名玩家开始之前，第一名玩家有 116 的机会在第一轮中被杀死。不幸的是，这是直觉错误的少数情况之一。一旦枪管旋转，子弹的位置就固定了。如果你先走，当且仅当子弹位于 1、3 和 5 室时，你就输了。所以你输的概率与第二个玩家相同，1/2。从这个意义上说，第一还是第二并不重要。

problems.titleEn: Now, let's change the rule slightly. We will spin the barrel again after every trigger pull. Will you choose to be the first or the second player?
problems.titleZh: 现在，让我们稍微改变一下规则。每次扣动扳机后，我们都会再次旋转枪管。你会选择成为第一名还是第二名玩家？
tags: Now, let's change the rule slightly. We will spin the barrel again after every trigger pull. Will you choose to be the first or the second player?
problems.promptEn: Now, let's change the rule slightly. We will spin the barrel again after every trigger pull. Will you choose to be the first or the second player? And what is your probability of loss?
problems.promptZh: 现在，让我们稍微改变一下规则。每次扣动扳机后，我们都会再次旋转枪管。你会选择成为第一名还是第二名玩家？您损失的概率是多少？
problems.answer: Choose to be the second player; your probability of losing is 5/11.
problems.answerEn: Choose to be the second player; your probability of losing is 5/11.
problems.answerZh: 选择第二个玩家；你的失败概率为 5/11。
problems.explanation:
Because the barrel is spun again after every trigger pull, each pull is independent.

Let p be the first player's probability of losing. On the first pull, the first player loses with probability 1/6. With probability 5/6 the first player survives, and the game is now in the same position with the roles reversed, so the first player's remaining probability of losing is 1-p.

Thus p = 1/6 + (5/6)(1-p). Solving gives p = 6/11. The second player's probability of losing is therefore 1-p = 5/11, so you should choose to be second.
problems.explanationEn:
Because the barrel is spun again after every trigger pull, each pull is independent.

Let p be the first player's probability of losing. On the first pull, the first player loses with probability 1/6. With probability 5/6 the first player survives, and the game is now in the same position with the roles reversed, so the first player's remaining probability of losing is 1-p.

Thus p = 1/6 + (5/6)(1-p). Solving gives p = 6/11. The second player's probability of losing is therefore 1-p = 5/11, so you should choose to be second.
problems.explanationZh:
因为每次扣动扳机后都会重新旋转弹巢，所以每次扣动都是独立的。

设第一个玩家失败的概率为 p。第一次扣动时，第一个玩家以 1/6 的概率失败。以 5/6 的概率他会幸存，此时角色互换，剩余游戏中他失败的概率为 1-p。

因此 p = 1/6 + (5/6)(1-p)，解得 p = 6/11。第二个玩家失败的概率为 1-p = 5/11，所以应该选择第二个玩家。

problems.titleEn: If instead of one bullet, two bullets are randomly put in the chamber. Your opponent playe
problems.titleZh: 如果不是一颗子弹，而是随机将两颗子弹放入腔室中。你的对手玩家
tags: If instead of one bullet, two bullets are randomly put in the chamber. Your opponent playe
problems.promptEn: If instead of one bullet, two bullets are randomly put in the chamber. Your opponent played the first and he was alive after the first trigger pull. You are given the option whether to spin the barrel. Should you spin the barrel?
problems.promptZh: 如果不是一颗子弹，而是随机将两颗子弹放入腔室中。你的对手先打，并且在第一次扣动扳机后他还活着。您可以选择是否旋转桶。你应该旋转桶吗？
problems.explanation: if you spin the barrel, the probability that you will lose in this round is 2/6. If you don't spin the barrel, there are only 5 chambers left and your probability oflosing in this round (conditioned on that your opponent survived) is 2/5. So you should spin the barrel.
problems.explanationEn: if you spin the barrel, the probability that you will lose in this round is 2/6. If you don't spin the barrel, there are only 5 chambers left and your probability oflosing in this round (conditioned on that your opponent survived) is 2/5. So you should spin the barrel.
problems.explanationZh: 如果你旋转木桶，你在这一轮中失败的概率是 2/6。如果你不旋转木桶，那么只剩下5个房间，你在这一轮中失败的概率（以你的对手幸存为条件）是2/5。所以你应该旋转桶。

problems.titleEn: What if the two bullets are randomly put in two consecutive positions?
problems.titleZh: 如果将两颗子弹随机放在两个连续的位置怎么办？
tags: What if the two bullets are randomly put in two consecutive positions?
problems.promptEn: What if the two bullets are randomly put in two consecutive positions? If your opponent survived his first round, should you spin the barrel?
problems.promptZh: 如果将两颗子弹随机放在两个连续的位置怎么办？如果你的对手在第一回合中幸存，你应该旋转木桶吗？
problems.explanation: Now we have to condition our probability on the fact that the positions of the two bullets are consecutive. As shown in Figure 4.3, let's label the empty chambers as 1, 2, 3 and 4; label the ones with bullets 5 and 6. Since your opponent survived the first round, the possible position he encountered is 1, 2, 3 or 4 with equal probability. With 1/4 chance, the next one is a bullet (the position was 4). So if you don't spin, the chance of survival is 3/4. If you spin the barrel, each position has equal probability of being chosen, and your chance of survival is only 2/3. So you should not spin the barrel. 0) Figure 4.3 Russian roulette with two consecutive bullets.
problems.explanationEn: Now we have to condition our probability on the fact that the positions of the two bullets are consecutive. As shown in Figure 4.3, let's label the empty chambers as 1, 2, 3 and 4; label the ones with bullets 5 and 6. Since your opponent survived the first round, the possible position he encountered is 1, 2, 3 or 4 with equal probability. With 1/4 chance, the next one is a bullet (the position was 4). So if you don't spin, the chance of survival is 3/4. If you spin the barrel, each position has equal probability of being chosen, and your chance of survival is only 2/3. So you should not spin the barrel. 0) Figure 4.3 Russian roulette with two consecutive bullets.
problems.explanationZh: 现在我们必须根据两个子弹的位置是连续的事实来确定我们的概率。如图4.3所示，我们将空室标记为1、2、3和4；将子弹标记为 5 和 6。由于你的对手在第一轮中幸存，因此他遇到的可能位置是 1、2、3 或 4，概率相同。有1/4的机会，下一颗是子弹（位置是4）。所以如果你不旋转，生存的机会是3/4。如果你旋转木桶，每个位置被选择的概率都是相等的，你的生存机会只有2/3。所以你不应该旋转桶。 0) 图 4.3 带有两个连续子弹的俄罗斯轮盘赌。

problems.promptEn: Fifty-two cards are randomly distributed to 4 players with each player getting 13 cards. What is the probability that each of them will have an ace?
problems.promptZh: 52 张牌随机分配给 4 名玩家，每位玩家获得 13 张牌。他们每个人都有 A 的概率是多少？
problems.explanation:
Reveal where the four aces are dealt. The first ace can be anywhere. For the second ace to be in a different player's hand, it must fall in one of the $39$ cards outside the first ace's 13-card hand, among the remaining $51$ card positions. Given that, for the third ace to be in a third hand, it must fall in one of $26$ positions among the remaining $50$. Finally, the fourth ace must fall in the last remaining player's $13$ positions among the remaining $49$.

Thus the probability is
$$1\cdot\frac{39}{51}\cdot\frac{26}{50}\cdot\frac{13}{49}=\frac{2197}{20825}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
Reveal where the four aces are dealt. The first ace can be anywhere. For the second ace to be in a different player's hand, it must fall in one of the $39$ cards outside the first ace's 13-card hand, among the remaining $51$ card positions. Given that, for the third ace to be in a third hand, it must fall in one of $26$ positions among the remaining $50$. Finally, the fourth ace must fall in the last remaining player's $13$ positions among the remaining $49$.

Thus the probability is
$$1\cdot\frac{39}{51}\cdot\frac{26}{50}\cdot\frac{13}{49}=\frac{2197}{20825}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
只需看四张 A 被发到哪里。第一张 A 可以在任意位置。第二张 A 要与第一张 A 在不同玩家手中，必须落在第一张 A 所在 13 张手牌之外的 $39$ 个位置中，而剩余位置共有 $51$ 个。在此条件下，第三张 A 要落在第三个玩家手中，必须落在剩余 $50$ 个位置中的 $26$ 个位置之一。最后，第四张 A 必须落在最后一个玩家剩余的 $13$ 个位置中，而总剩余位置为 $49$。

因此概率为
$$1\cdot\frac{39}{51}\cdot\frac{26}{50}\cdot\frac{13}{49}=\frac{2197}{20825}.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Counting expression was truncated; answer fields were empty.

problems.promptEn: A gambler starts with an initial fortune of i dollars. On each successive game, the gambler wins \$1 with probability p, 0 < p < I, or loses \$1 with probability q = 1- p . He will stop if he either accumulates N dollars or loses all his money. What is the probability that he will end up with N dollars?
problems.promptZh: 一名赌徒初始有 i 美元。每局游戏中，他以概率 p（0 < p < 1）赢得 1 美元，以概率 q = 1 - p 输掉 1 美元。当他的财富达到 N 美元或变为 0 时停止。求他最终达到 N 美元的概率。
problems.answerZh: 当 p != q 时为 (1-(q/p)^i)/(1-(q/p)^N)；当 p=q=1/2 时为 i/N
problems.explanation:
Let $P_i$ be the probability of reaching $N$ before $0$ when starting with $i$ dollars. Then
$$P_i=pP_{i+1}+qP_{i-1},\qquad P_0=0,\quad P_N=1.$$
For $p\neq q$, solving this second-order difference equation gives
$$P_i=\frac{1-(q/p)^i}{1-(q/p)^N}.$$
For the fair case $p=q=1/2$, the solution is linear:
$$P_i=\frac{i}{N}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
Let $P_i$ be the probability of reaching $N$ before $0$ when starting with $i$ dollars. Then
$$P_i=pP_{i+1}+qP_{i-1},\qquad P_0=0,\quad P_N=1.$$
For $p\neq q$, solving this second-order difference equation gives
$$P_i=\frac{1-(q/p)^i}{1-(q/p)^N}.$$
For the fair case $p=q=1/2$, the solution is linear:
$$P_i=\frac{i}{N}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
令 $P_i$ 表示从 $i$ 美元出发，先达到 $N$ 而不是先到 $0$ 的概率。则
$$P_i=pP_{i+1}+qP_{i-1},\qquad P_0=0,\quad P_N=1.$$
当 $p\neq q$ 时，解这个二阶差分方程得到
$$P_i=\frac{1-(q/p)^i}{1-(q/p)^N}.$$
当公平情形 $p=q=1/2$ 时，解为线性函数：
$$P_i=\frac{i}{N}.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Gambler's ruin formula was garbled and Chinese prompt was mistranslated.

problems.promptEn: A basketball player is taking 100 free throws. She scores one point if the ball passes through the hoop and zero point if she misses. She has scored on her first throw and missed on her second. For each of the following throw the probability of her scoring is the fraction of throws she has made so far. For example, if she has scored 23 points after the 40th throw, the probability that she will score in the 41 th throw is 23/40. After 100 throws (including the first and the second), what is the probability that she scores exactly 50 baskets?
problems.promptZh: 一名篮球运动员罚球 100 次。球进得 1 分，没进得 0 分。她第一次罚球命中，第二次罚球未中。之后每次罚球命中的概率等于她到目前为止的命中比例。例如，如果第 40 次罚球后她命中 23 次，则第 41 次命中的概率为 23/40。100 次罚球后（包括前两次），她恰好命中 50 次的概率是多少？
problems.explanation:
After the first two throws, the process is the classic Pólya urn with one success and one failure. Let $P_{n,k}$ be the probability of having $k$ successes after $n$ throws. We claim that for $n\geq2$,
$$P_{n,k}=\frac{1}{n-1},\qquad k=1,2,\ldots,n-1.$$
This is true for $n=2$. If it holds at time $n$, then
$$P_{n+1,k}=P_{n,k}\left(1-\frac{k}{n}\right)+P_{n,k-1}\frac{k-1}{n}
=\frac{1}{n-1}\cdot\frac{n-k}{n}+\frac{1}{n-1}\cdot\frac{k-1}{n}
=\frac{1}{n}.$$
Thus the number of successes after 100 throws is uniform over $1,2,\ldots,99$, so
$$P_{100,50}=\frac{1}{99}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
After the first two throws, the process is the classic Pólya urn with one success and one failure. Let $P_{n,k}$ be the probability of having $k$ successes after $n$ throws. We claim that for $n\geq2$,
$$P_{n,k}=\frac{1}{n-1},\qquad k=1,2,\ldots,n-1.$$
This is true for $n=2$. If it holds at time $n$, then
$$P_{n+1,k}=P_{n,k}\left(1-\frac{k}{n}\right)+P_{n,k-1}\frac{k-1}{n}
=\frac{1}{n-1}\cdot\frac{n-k}{n}+\frac{1}{n-1}\cdot\frac{k-1}{n}
=\frac{1}{n}.$$
Thus the number of successes after 100 throws is uniform over $1,2,\ldots,99$, so
$$P_{100,50}=\frac{1}{99}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
前两次罚球之后，这个过程等价于经典的 Pólya urn：初始有一个成功和一个失败。令 $P_{n,k}$ 表示 $n$ 次罚球后共有 $k$ 次命中的概率。我们断言对 $n\geq2$，
$$P_{n,k}=\frac{1}{n-1},\qquad k=1,2,\ldots,n-1.$$
这在 $n=2$ 时显然成立。若在 $n$ 时刻成立，则
$$P_{n+1,k}=P_{n,k}\left(1-\frac{k}{n}\right)+P_{n,k-1}\frac{k-1}{n}
=\frac{1}{n-1}\cdot\frac{n-k}{n}+\frac{1}{n-1}\cdot\frac{k-1}{n}
=\frac{1}{n}.$$
因此 100 次罚球后的命中次数在 $1,2,\ldots,99$ 上均匀分布，所以
$$P_{100,50}=\frac{1}{99}.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Pólya urn induction notation and final probability were OCR-corrupted.

problems.promptEn: If the probability of observing at least one car on a highway during any 20-minute time interval is 609/625, then what is the probability of observing at least one car during any 5-minute time interval? Assume that the probability of seeing a car at any moment is uniform (constant) for the entire 20 minutes.
problems.promptZh: 如果在任何 20 分钟的时间间隔内观察到高速公路上至少一辆汽车的概率为 609/625，那么在任何 5 分钟的时间间隔内观察到至少一辆汽车的概率是多少？假设任意时刻看到汽车的概率在整个 20 分钟内是均匀的（恒定）。
problems.answer: The probability is 3/5.
problems.answerEn: The probability is 3/5.
problems.answerZh: 概率为 3/5。
problems.explanation:
Split the 20-minute interval into four independent 5-minute intervals with the same probability p of seeing at least one car.

The probability of seeing no cars in a 5-minute interval is 1-p, so the probability of seeing no cars in all four intervals is (1-p)^4.

Since the probability of seeing at least one car in 20 minutes is 609/625, the probability of seeing no cars in 20 minutes is 1-609/625=16/625.

Thus (1-p)^4=16/625=(2/5)^4, so 1-p=2/5 and p=3/5.
problems.explanationEn:
Split the 20-minute interval into four independent 5-minute intervals with the same probability p of seeing at least one car.

The probability of seeing no cars in a 5-minute interval is 1-p, so the probability of seeing no cars in all four intervals is (1-p)^4.

Since the probability of seeing at least one car in 20 minutes is 609/625, the probability of seeing no cars in 20 minutes is 1-609/625=16/625.

Thus (1-p)^4=16/625=(2/5)^4, so 1-p=2/5 and p=3/5.
problems.explanationZh:
把 20 分钟分成四个不重叠的 5 分钟区间。设任意 5 分钟内至少看到一辆车的概率为 p。

那么一个 5 分钟区间内看不到车的概率为 1-p，四个区间都看不到车的概率为 (1-p)^4。

已知 20 分钟内至少看到一辆车的概率为 609/625，所以 20 分钟内看不到车的概率为 1-609/625=16/625。

因此 (1-p)^4=16/625=(2/5)^4，得到 1-p=2/5，故 p=3/5。

problems.promptEn: Two bankers each arrive at the station at some random time between 5:00 am and 6:00 am (arrival time for either banker is uniformly distributed). They stay exactly five minutes and then leave. What is the probability they will meet on a given day?
problems.promptZh: 两名银行家分别在上午 5:00 至 6:00 之间的某个随机时间到达车站（任一银行家的到达时间是均匀分布的）。他们足足停留了五分钟，然后就离开了。他们在某一天见面的概率是多少？

problems.promptEn: A stick is cut twice randomly (each cut point follows a uniform distribution on the stick), what is the probability that the 3 segments can form a triangle?
problems.promptZh: 一根木棍被随机切割两次（每个切割点在木棍上均匀分布），这 3 段可以形成三角形的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: Without loss of generality, let's assume that the length of the stick is 1. Let's also label the point of the first cut as x and the second cut as y . If x < y, then the three segments are x,y-x and 1-y. The conditions to form a triangle are A ¥ x y-x x+(y-x) > 1- y => y > 112 x + (1- y) > y - x => y < 1I2 + x (y- x) + (1- y) > x => x < 112 The feasible area is shown in Figure 4.5. The y case for x < y is the left gray triangle. Using symmetry, we can see that the case for x > y is X the right gray triangle. Figure 4.5 Distribution of cuts X and Y The total shadowed area represents the region where 3 segments can form a triangle, which is 1/4 of the square. So the probability is 114.

The y case for x < y is the left gray triangle. Using symmetry, we can see that the case for x > y is X the right gray triangle. Figure 4.5 Distribution of cuts X and Y The total shadowed area represents the region where 3 segments can form a triangle, which is 1/4 of the square. So the probability is 114.
problems.explanationZh: 不失一般性，我们假设棒的长度为 1。我们还将第一次切割的点标记为 x ，将第二次切割的点标记为 y 。如果 x < y，则这三个线段是 x、y-x 和 1-y。形成三角形的条件为 A ¥ x y-x x+(y-x) > 1- y => y > 112 x + (1- y) > y - x => y < 1I2 + x (y- x) + (1- y) > x => x < 112 可行面积如图 4.5 所示。 x < y 的 y 情况是左侧灰色三角形。利用对称性，我们可以看到 x > y 的情况是 X 是灰色直角三角形。图 4.5 切割 X 和 Y 的分布 总阴影面积表示 3 个线段可以形成三角形的区域，即正方形的 1/4。所以概率是114。

problems.promptEn: You are waiting for a bus at a bus station. The buses arrive at the station according to a Poisson process with an average arrival time of 10 minutes (.A = 0.1 I min). If the buses have been running for a long time and you arrive at the bus station at a random time, what is your expected waiting time? On average, how many minutes ago did the last bus leave?
problems.promptZh: 您正在公交车站等公交车。公交车按照泊松过程到达车站，平均到达时间为 10 分钟（.A = 0.1 I 分钟）。如果公交车已经运行了很长时间，并且您在随机时间到达公交车站，您预计等待的时间是多少？平均而言，末班车是在多少分钟前出发？
problems.explanation: Considering the importance of jump-diffusion processes in derivative pricing and the role of Poisson processes in studying jump processes, let's elaborate more on exponential random variables and the Poison process. Exponential distribution is widely used to model the time interval between independent events that happen at a constant ( . 1 ) 1 /() \{Ae-"' (t 0) Th d . 1 . . 1/ 1 average rate arr1va rate 11.: t = . e expecte amva time is /L (t < 0) and the variance is 1IA2 • Using integration, we can calculate the cdf of an exponential distribution to be F(t) = P(r s; t) = l-e-'1 and P(r > t) = e_,,, where r is the random variable for arrival time. One unique property of exponential distribution is memorylessness: P \{ r > s + t I r > s\} = P( r > t\}. 23 That means if we have waited for s time units, the extra waiting time has the same distribution as the waiting time when we start at time 0. When the arrivals of a series of events each independently follow an exponential distribution with arrival rate A., the number of arrivals between time 0 and t can be -A.I At' modeled as a Poisson process P(N(t) = x) = e , x = 0, 1, \cdot  \cdot  \cdot  24 The expected x! number of arrivals is At and the variance is also At. Because of the memoryless nature of exponential distribution, the number of arrivals between time s and t is also a Poisson e-.l(t-s) ( A(t-S) r process x! Taking advantage of the memoryless property of exponential distribution, we know that the expected waiting time is 1 I A = 10 min. If you look back in time, the memoryless property stills applies. So on average, the last bus arrived 10 minutes ago as well. 23 P\{r > s+t Ir> s\} = e-.<(s+i> I e--<.• = e_,., = P(x > t\} This is another example that your intuition may misguide you. You may be wondering that if the last bus on average arrived 10 minutes ago and the next bus on average will arrive 10 minutes later, shouldn't the average arrival time be 20 minutes instead of 10? The explanation to the apparent discrepancy is that when you arrive at a random time, you are more likely to arrive in a long time interval between two bus arrivals than in a short one. For example, if one interval between two bus arrivals is 30 minutes and another is 5 minutes, you are more likely to arrive at a time during that 30-minute interval rather than 5-minute interval. In fact, if you arrive at a random time, the E[X 2 ] expected residual life (the time for the next bus to arrive) is for a general 2E[X] distribution.

problems.explanationZh: 考虑到跳跃扩散过程在衍生品定价中的重要性以及泊松过程在研究跳跃过程中的作用，让我们详细说明指数随机变量和泊松过程。指数分布广泛用于对以常数 ( . 1 ) 1 /() \{Ae-"' (t 0) Th d . 1 . . 1/ 1 平均速率 arr1va 速率 11.: t = . e 预期 amva 时间为 /L (t < 0) 且方差为 1IA2 发生的独立事件之间的时间间隔进行建模 • 使用积分，我们可以计算指数分布的 cdf F(t) = P(r s; t) = l-e-'1 且 P(r > t) = e_,,,，其中 r 是到达时间的随机变量，指数分布的一个独特属性是无记忆性： P \{ r > s + t I r > s\} = P( r > t\}. 23 这意味着如果我们等待了 s 个时间单位，则额外的等待时间与我们在 时刻 开始时的等待时间具有相同的分布。 0. 当一系列事件的到达均独立地服从到达率 A 的指数分布时，时间 0 和 t 之间的到达次数可以用 -A.I At' 建模为泊松过程 P(N(t) = x) = e , x = 0, 1, \cdot \cdot \cdot 24 预期的 x! 到达次数为 At，方差也为 At。指数分布的无记忆特性，时间 s 和 t 之间的到达次数也是泊松 e-.l(t-s) ( A(t-S) r 过程 x！利用指数分布的无记忆特性，我们知道预期等待时间是 1 I A = 10 分钟。如果你回顾过去，无记忆特性仍然适用。所以平均而言，最后一班车也是 10 分钟前到达的。23 P\{r > s+t Ir> s\} = e-.<(s+i> I e--<.• = e_,., = P(x > t\} 这又是一个你的直觉可能会误导你的例子。你可能会想，如果末班车平均在 10 分钟前到达，下一班车平均在 10 分钟后到达，那么平均到达时间不应该是 20 分钟而不是 10 分钟吗？对这种明显差异的解释是，当你到达例如，如果两次公交车到达之间的时间间隔是 30 分钟，另一次是 5 分钟，那么您更有可能在 30 分钟间隔内到达，而不是在 5 分钟间隔内到达。事实上，如果您在随机时间到达，则 E[X 2 ] 预期剩余寿命（下一班车到达的时间）适用于一般 2E[X] 分布。

problems.promptEn: You have I 00 noodles in your soup bowl. Being blindfolded, you are told to take two ends of some noodles (each end on any noodle has the same probability of being chosen) in your bowl and connect them. You continue until there are no free ends. The number of loops formed by the noodles this way is stochastic. Calculate the expected number of circles.
problems.promptZh: 你的汤碗里有00面条。被蒙住眼睛后，你被告知将一些面条的两端（任何面条的每一端被选中的概率相同）放入碗中并将它们连接起来。你继续下去，直到没有自由端。面条以这种方式形成的环的数量是随机的。计算预期的圆数。
problems.explanation: Again do not be frightened by the large number 100. If you have no clue how to start, let's begin with the simplest case where n =I. Surely you have only one choice (to connect both ends of the noodle), so E[/(l)] =I. How about 2 noodles? Now you (4) 4 x 3 have 4 ends ( 2 x 2) and you can connect any two of them. There are 2 = - 2- = 6 combinations. Among them, 2 combinations will connect both ends of the same noodle together and yield I circle and I noodle. The other 4 choices will yield a single noodle. So the expected number of circles is £[/(2)] = 2/ 6 x (1+£[/(I)])+416 x E[/(l)] =I I 3 +£[/(I)]= I I 3+ 1. We now move on to 3 noodles with ( = 6 ; 5 = I 5 choices. Among them, 3 choices will yield 1 circle and 2 noodles; the other 12 choices will yield 2 noodles only, so E[/(3)] = 3/15 x (I+ £[/(2)]) + 12/15x E[/(2)] = 115+ E[/(2)] = 115+1/3+1. See the pattern? For any n noodles, we will have £[/ (n)] = 1+1I3+1I5 +\cdot \cdot \cdot +I /(2n -1), which can be easily proved by induction. Plug I 00 in, we will have the answer. Actually after the 2-noodle case, you probably have found the key to this question. If you start with n noodles, among ( 22n) = n ( 2 n - 1) possible combinations, we have n = - 1- probability to yield 1 circle and n - I noodles and 2n - 2 probability n(2n-I) 2n-12n-l to yield n-1 noodles only, so E[f(n)] = E[/(n-1)]+-1- . Working backward, you 2n-1 can get the final solution as well.

problems.promptEn: You just bought one share of stock A and want to hedge it by shorting stock B. How many shares of B should you short to minimize the variance of the hedged position? Assume that the variance of stock A's return is o-3; the variance of B's return is a;; their correlation coefficient is p.
problems.promptZh: 您刚刚购买了一股股票 A，并希望通过做空股票 B 对其进行对冲。您应该做空股票 B 的多少股才能最大限度地减少对冲头寸的方差？假设股票A的收益方差为o-3； B 的回报的方差是 a;;它们的相关系数为p。
problems.explanation: Suppose that we short h shares of B, the variance of the portfolio return is var(rA -hrs)= o-3 -2phaAas + h2a; The best hedge ratio should minimize var(rA -hrs)\cdot  Take the first order partial derivative with respect to hand set it to zero: ovar = -2paAas +2ha; =0 h = p aA . Oh O"s To confirm it's the minimum, we can also check the second-order partial derivative: 82 var - 2o-2 > O So Indeed when h =pa A, the hedge portfolio has the minimum Oh2 - S • O"s variance.

Oh O"s To confirm it's the minimum, we can also check the second-order partial derivative: 82 var - 2o-2 > O So Indeed when h =pa A, the hedge portfolio has the minimum Oh2 - S • O"s variance.
problems.explanationZh: 假设我们做空B股的h股，则投资组合收益的方差为var(rA -hrs)= o-3 -2phaAas + h2a；最佳的对冲比率应最小化 var(rA -hrs)\cdot 对手部取一阶偏导数并将其设置为零：ovar = -2paAas +2ha； =0 h = p aA 。 Oh O"s 为了确认它是最小值，我们还可以检查二阶偏导数： 82 var - 2o-2 > O 所以事实上，当 h =pa A 时，对冲投资组合具有最小 Oh2 - S • O"s 方差。

problems.promptEn: Suppose that you roll a die. For each roll, you are paid the face value. If the roll is 4, 5, or 6, you may roll again. Once you roll 1, 2, or 3, the game stops. What is the expected payoff of this game?
problems.promptZh: 假设你掷一枚骰子。每次掷出后，你获得等于点数的报酬。如果掷出 4、5 或 6，你可以继续掷；一旦掷出 1、2 或 3，游戏停止。这个游戏的期望收益是多少？
problems.explanation:
Let $E$ be the expected payoff. On the first roll, with probability $1/2$ the result is in $\{1,2,3\}$ and the game stops; the conditional average payoff is $2$. With probability $1/2$ the result is in $\{4,5,6\}$; the conditional average immediate payoff is $5$, and then the game starts over with expected value $E$.

Thus
$$E=\frac12\cdot2+\frac12(5+E).$$
Solving gives
$$E=7.$$

With probability $1/2$ the result is in $\{4,5,6\}$; the conditional average immediate payoff is $5$, and then the game starts over with expected value $E$.

Thus
$$E=\frac12\cdot2+\frac12(5+E).$$
Solving gives
$$E=7.$$
problems.explanationZh:
令 $E$ 为期望收益。第一次掷骰时，以概率 $1/2$ 得到 $\{1,2,3\}$ 中的点数并停止；此时条件平均收益为 $2$。以概率 $1/2$ 得到 $\{4,5,6\}$ 中的点数；此时条件平均即时收益为 $5$，然后游戏重新开始，额外期望仍为 $E$。

因此
$$E=\frac12\cdot2+\frac12(5+E).$$
解得
$$E=7.$$

problems.promptEn: What is the expected number of cards that need to be turned over in a regular 52-card deck in order to see the first ace?
problems.promptZh: 在常规 52 张牌中，为了看到第一张 A，预计需要翻开多少张牌？
problems.explanation:
There are $4$ aces and $48$ non-aces. For each non-ace card $i$, let $X_i=1$ if it appears before all four aces, and $X_i=0$ otherwise. The number of cards turned over until the first ace appears is
$$X=1+\sum_{i=1}^{48}X_i.$$
In a random ordering, a fixed non-ace is equally likely to fall in any of the five gaps created by the four aces. It is before all four aces with probability $1/5$. Therefore
$$\mathbb{E}[X]=1+48\cdot\frac15=\frac{53}{5}=10.6.$$
problems.explanationEn:
There are $4$ aces and $48$ non-aces. For each non-ace card $i$, let $X_i=1$ if it appears before all four aces, and $X_i=0$ otherwise. The number of cards turned over until the first ace appears is
$$X=1+\sum_{i=1}^{48}X_i.$$
In a random ordering, a fixed non-ace is equally likely to fall in any of the five gaps created by the four aces. It is before all four aces with probability $1/5$. Therefore
$$\mathbb{E}[X]=1+48\cdot\frac15=\frac{53}{5}=10.6.$$
problems.explanationZh:
共有 $4$ 张 A 和 $48$ 张非 A。对每张非 A 牌 $i$，令 $X_i=1$ 表示它出现在四张 A 之前，否则 $X_i=0$。翻到第一张 A 所需的牌数为
$$X=1+\sum_{i=1}^{48}X_i.$$
在随机排列中，固定的一张非 A 等可能落在四张 A 分出的五个间隔之一。它出现在所有 A 之前的概率为 $1/5$。因此
$$\mathbb{E}[X]=1+48\cdot\frac15=\frac{53}{5}=10.6.$$

problems.promptEn: If there is a 50% probability that bond A will default next year and a 30% probability that bond B will default. What is the range of probability that at least one bond defaults and what is the range of their correlation?
problems.promptZh: 如果明年债券 A 违约的概率为 50%，债券 B 违约的概率为 30%。至少一种债券违约的概率范围是多少？它们的相关性范围是多少？
problems.explanation:
Let $p_A=0.5$, $p_B=0.3$, and let $p_{AB}=P(A\cap B)$. Feasible joint probabilities satisfy
$$0\leq p_{AB}\leq \min(p_A,p_B)=0.3.$$
Since
$$P(A\cup B)=p_A+p_B-p_{AB}=0.8-p_{AB},$$
the union probability ranges from $0.5$ to $0.8$.

For the correlation of default indicators $I_A$ and $I_B$,
$$\rho=\frac{\operatorname{Cov}(I_A,I_B)}{\sqrt{\operatorname{Var}(I_A)\operatorname{Var}(I_B)}}
=\frac{p_{AB}-0.5\cdot0.3}{\sqrt{0.5(0.5)\cdot0.3(0.7)}}.$$
The denominator is $\sqrt{0.0525}=\sqrt{21}/20$. As $p_{AB}$ ranges from $0$ to $0.3$, the covariance ranges from $-0.15$ to $0.15$. Hence
$$\rho\in\left[-\frac{3}{\sqrt{21}},\frac{3}{\sqrt{21}}\right]
=\left[-\sqrt{\frac37},\sqrt{\frac37}\right].$$

Since
$$P(A\cup B)=p_A+p_B-p_{AB}=0.8-p_{AB},$$
the union probability ranges from $0.5$ to $0.8$.

For the correlation of default indicators $I_A$ and $I_B$,
$$\rho=\frac{\operatorname{Cov}(I_A,I_B)}{\sqrt{\operatorname{Var}(I_A)\operatorname{Var}(I_B)}}
=\frac{p_{AB}-0.5\cdot0.3}{\sqrt{0.5(0.5)\cdot0.3(0.7)}}.$$
The denominator is $\sqrt{0.0525}=\sqrt{21}/20$. As $p_{AB}$ ranges from $0$ to $0.3$, the covariance ranges from $-0.15$ to $0.15$. Hence

=\left[-\sqrt{\frac37},\sqrt{\frac37}\right].$$
problems.explanationZh:
令 $p_A=0.5$、$p_B=0.3$，并令 $p_{AB}=P(A\cap B)$。可行的联合概率满足
$$0\leq p_{AB}\leq \min(p_A,p_B)=0.3.$$
由于
$$P(A\cup B)=p_A+p_B-p_{AB}=0.8-p_{AB},$$
所以至少一个债券违约的概率范围为 $0.5$ 到 $0.8$。

对违约指示变量 $I_A$ 和 $I_B$，相关系数为
$$\rho=\frac{\operatorname{Cov}(I_A,I_B)}{\sqrt{\operatorname{Var}(I_A)\operatorname{Var}(I_B)}}
=\frac{p_{AB}-0.5\cdot0.3}{\sqrt{0.5(0.5)\cdot0.3(0.7)}}.$$
分母为 $\sqrt{0.0525}=\sqrt{21}/20$。当 $p_{AB}$ 从 $0$ 到 $0.3$ 变化时，协方差从 $-0.15$ 到 $0.15$。因此
$$\rho\in\left[-\frac{3}{\sqrt{21}},\frac{3}{\sqrt{21}}\right]
=\left[-\sqrt{\frac37},\sqrt{\frac37}\right].$$

problems.titleEn: Expected value of max and min
tags: Expected value of max and min
problems.promptEn:
Let $X_1,X_2,\ldots,X_n$ be IID uniform random variables on $[0,1]$. What are the CDF, PDF, and expected value of
$$Z_n=\max(X_1,\ldots,X_n)?$$
What are the CDF, PDF, and expected value of
$$Y_n=\min(X_1,\ldots,X_n)?$$
problems.promptZh:
令 $X_1,X_2,\ldots,X_n$ 为 $[0,1]$ 上的独立同分布均匀随机变量。求
$$Z_n=\max(X_1,\ldots,X_n)$$
的累积分布函数、概率密度函数和期望。再求
$$Y_n=\min(X_1,\ldots,X_n)$$
的累积分布函数、概率密度函数和期望。
problems.answer: For $Z_n=\max_i X_i$: $F_Z(z)=z^n$, $f_Z(z)=nz^{n-1}$, $\mathbb{E}[Z_n]=n/(n+1)$. For $Y_n=\min_i X_i$: $F_Y(y)=1-(1-y)^n$, $f_Y(y)=n(1-y)^{n-1}$, $\mathbb{E}[Y_n]=1/(n+1)$ on $0\leq y,z\leq1$.
problems.answerEn: For $Z_n=\max_i X_i$: $F_Z(z)=z^n$, $f_Z(z)=nz^{n-1}$, $\mathbb{E}[Z_n]=n/(n+1)$. For $Y_n=\min_i X_i$: $F_Y(y)=1-(1-y)^n$, $f_Y(y)=n(1-y)^{n-1}$, $\mathbb{E}[Y_n]=1/(n+1)$ on $0\leq y,z\leq1$.
problems.answerZh: 对 $Z_n=\max_i X_i$：$F_Z(z)=z^n$，$f_Z(z)=nz^{n-1}$，$\mathbb{E}[Z_n]=n/(n+1)$。对 $Y_n=\min_i X_i$：$F_Y(y)=1-(1-y)^n$，$f_Y(y)=n(1-y)^{n-1}$，$\mathbb{E}[Y_n]=1/(n+1)$，其中 $0\leq y,z\leq1$。
problems.explanation:
For $0\leq z\leq1$,
$$F_Z(z)=P(Z_n\leq z)=P(X_1\leq z,\ldots,X_n\leq z)=z^n.$$
Thus
$$f_Z(z)=nz^{n-1},\qquad \mathbb{E}[Z_n]=\int_0^1 z\,nz^{n-1}\,dz=\frac{n}{n+1}.$$

For $0\leq y\leq1$,
$$P(Y_n>y)=P(X_1>y,\ldots,X_n>y)=(1-y)^n.$$
Therefore
$$F_Y(y)=1-(1-y)^n,\qquad f_Y(y)=n(1-y)^{n-1},$$
and
$$\mathbb{E}[Y_n]=\int_0^1 y\,n(1-y)^{n-1}\,dy=\frac{1}{n+1}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
For $0\leq z\leq1$,
$$F_Z(z)=P(Z_n\leq z)=P(X_1\leq z,\ldots,X_n\leq z)=z^n.$$
Thus
$$f_Z(z)=nz^{n-1},\qquad \mathbb{E}[Z_n]=\int_0^1 z\,nz^{n-1}\,dz=\frac{n}{n+1}.$$

For $0\leq y\leq1$,
$$P(Y_n>y)=P(X_1>y,\ldots,X_n>y)=(1-y)^n.$$
Therefore
$$F_Y(y)=1-(1-y)^n,\qquad f_Y(y)=n(1-y)^{n-1},$$
and
$$\mathbb{E}[Y_n]=\int_0^1 y\,n(1-y)^{n-1}\,dy=\frac{1}{n+1}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
当 $0\leq z\leq1$ 时，
$$F_Z(z)=P(Z_n\leq z)=P(X_1\leq z,\ldots,X_n\leq z)=z^n.$$
因此
$$f_Z(z)=nz^{n-1},\qquad \mathbb{E}[Z_n]=\int_0^1 z\,nz^{n-1}\,dz=\frac{n}{n+1}.$$

当 $0\leq y\leq1$ 时，
$$P(Y_n>y)=P(X_1>y,\ldots,X_n>y)=(1-y)^n.$$
所以
$$F_Y(y)=1-(1-y)^n,\qquad f_Y(y)=n(1-y)^{n-1},$$
并且
$$\mathbb{E}[Y_n]=\int_0^1 y\,n(1-y)^{n-1}\,dy=\frac{1}{n+1}.$$

problems.titleZh: 最大值和最小值的相关性
problems.promptEn:
Let $X_1$ and $X_2$ be IID uniform random variables on $[0,1]$, and define
$$Y=\min(X_1,X_2),\qquad Z=\max(X_1,X_2).$$
For $y,z\in[0,1]$, what is $P(Y\geq y\mid Z\leq z)$? What is the correlation of $Y$ and $Z$?
problems.promptZh:
令 $X_1$ 和 $X_2$ 为 $[0,1]$ 上的独立同分布均匀随机变量，并定义
$$Y=\min(X_1,X_2),\qquad Z=\max(X_1,X_2).$$
对 $y,z\in[0,1]$，求 $P(Y\geq y\mid Z\leq z)$。并求 $Y$ 和 $Z$ 的相关系数。
problems.answer: For $0\leq y\leq z\leq1$, $P(Y\geq y\mid Z\leq z)=((z-y)/z)^2$; it is $0$ if $y>z$. The correlation is $\operatorname{Corr}(Y,Z)=1/2$.
problems.answerEn: For $0\leq y\leq z\leq1$, $P(Y\geq y\mid Z\leq z)=((z-y)/z)^2$; it is $0$ if $y>z$. The correlation is $\operatorname{Corr}(Y,Z)=1/2$.
problems.answerZh: 当 $0\leq y\leq z\leq1$ 时，$P(Y\geq y\mid Z\leq z)=((z-y)/z)^2$；若 $y>z$ 则为 $0$。相关系数为 $\operatorname{Corr}(Y,Z)=1/2$。
problems.explanation:
The event $Z\leq z$ means both $X_1$ and $X_2$ lie in the square $[0,z]^2$, whose area is $z^2$. If $0\leq y\leq z$, then $Y\geq y$ additionally requires both coordinates to be at least $y$, giving the square $[y,z]^2$ with area $(z-y)^2$. Hence
$$P(Y\geq y\mid Z\leq z)=\frac{(z-y)^2}{z^2}.$$
If $y>z$, this conditional probability is $0$.

From the order-statistic formulas for two uniforms,
$$E[Y]=\frac13,\quad E[Z]=\frac23,\quad \operatorname{Var}(Y)=\operatorname{Var}(Z)=\frac1{18}.$$
Also $YZ=X_1X_2$ because the product of the minimum and maximum equals the product of the two original values. Thus
$$E[YZ]=E[X_1X_2]=E[X_1]E[X_2]=\frac14.$$
So
$$\operatorname{Cov}(Y,Z)=\frac14-\frac13\cdot\frac23=\frac1{36},$$
and
$$\operatorname{Corr}(Y,Z)=\frac{1/36}{1/18}=\frac12.$$
problems.explanationEn:
The event $Z\leq z$ means both $X_1$ and $X_2$ lie in the square $[0,z]^2$, whose area is $z^2$. If $0\leq y\leq z$, then $Y\geq y$ additionally requires both coordinates to be at least $y$, giving the square $[y,z]^2$ with area $(z-y)^2$. Hence
$$P(Y\geq y\mid Z\leq z)=\frac{(z-y)^2}{z^2}.$$
If $y>z$, this conditional probability is $0$.

From the order-statistic formulas for two uniforms,
$$E[Y]=\frac13,\quad E[Z]=\frac23,\quad \operatorname{Var}(Y)=\operatorname{Var}(Z)=\frac1{18}.$$
Also $YZ=X_1X_2$ because the product of the minimum and maximum equals the product of the two original values. Thus
$$E[YZ]=E[X_1X_2]=E[X_1]E[X_2]=\frac14.$$
So
$$\operatorname{Cov}(Y,Z)=\frac14-\frac13\cdot\frac23=\frac1{36},$$
and
$$\operatorname{Corr}(Y,Z)=\frac{1/36}{1/18}=\frac12.$$
problems.explanationZh:
事件 $Z\leq z$ 表示 $X_1$ 和 $X_2$ 都落在正方形 $[0,z]^2$ 中，其面积为 $z^2$。如果 $0\leq y\leq z$，则 $Y\geq y$ 还要求两个坐标都至少为 $y$，对应正方形 $[y,z]^2$，面积为 $(z-y)^2$。因此
$$P(Y\geq y\mid Z\leq z)=\frac{(z-y)^2}{z^2}.$$
若 $y>z$，该条件概率为 $0$。

由两个均匀变量的顺序统计量公式，
$$E[Y]=\frac13,\quad E[Z]=\frac23,\quad \operatorname{Var}(Y)=\operatorname{Var}(Z)=\frac1{18}.$$
并且 $YZ=X_1X_2$，因为最大值和最小值的乘积等于两个原变量的乘积。因此
$$E[YZ]=E[X_1X_2]=E[X_1]E[X_2]=\frac14.$$
所以
$$\operatorname{Cov}(Y,Z)=\frac14-\frac13\cdot\frac23=\frac1{36},$$
进而
$$\operatorname{Corr}(Y,Z)=\frac{1/36}{1/18}=\frac12.$$

problems.promptEn: 500 ants are randomly put on a 1-foot string, independently and uniformly between 0 and 1. Each ant randomly moves left or right with equal probability at speed 1 foot per minute until it falls off an end. Ants are infinitesimally small. When two ants collide head-on, they both immediately change directions and keep moving at 1 foot per minute. What is the expected time for all ants to fall off the string?
problems.promptZh: 500 只蚂蚁被随机放在一根 1 英尺长的绳子上，每只蚂蚁的位置在 0 到 1 之间独立均匀分布。每只蚂蚁以 1 英尺/分钟的速度等概率向左或向右移动，直到从某一端掉下。假设蚂蚁无限小。两只蚂蚁正面相撞时会立即改变方向并继续以 1 英尺/分钟移动。所有蚂蚁掉下绳子的期望时间是多少？
problems.answer: The expected time is 500/501 minutes, about 0.998 minutes.
problems.answerEn: The expected time is 500/501 minutes, about 0.998 minutes.
problems.answerZh: 期望时间为 500/501 分钟，约为 0.998 分钟。
problems.explanation:
When two ants collide and reverse directions, this is equivalent to the two ants passing through each other while exchanging labels. Since the ants are otherwise indistinguishable, collisions do not change the set of fall-off times.

For one ant initially at position X uniformly distributed on [0,1], the time to fall is X if it moves left and 1-X if it moves right. This time is uniformly distributed on [0,1].

Therefore the time until all 500 ants have fallen is the maximum of 500 IID Uniform(0,1) variables. If M is this maximum, P(M <= x)=x^500, so E[M]=500/501. The expected time is 500/501 minutes.
problems.explanationEn:
When two ants collide and reverse directions, this is equivalent to the two ants passing through each other while exchanging labels. Since the ants are otherwise indistinguishable, collisions do not change the set of fall-off times.

For one ant initially at position X uniformly distributed on [0,1], the time to fall is X if it moves left and 1-X if it moves right. This time is uniformly distributed on [0,1].

Therefore the time until all 500 ants have fallen is the maximum of 500 IID Uniform(0,1) variables. If M is this maximum, P(M <= x)=x^500, so E[M]=500/501. The expected time is 500/501 minutes.
problems.explanationZh:
两只蚂蚁相撞后同时掉头，等价于它们直接穿过彼此但交换标签。由于蚂蚁本身不可区分，相撞不会改变所有蚂蚁掉下绳子的时间集合。

一只蚂蚁初始位置 X 在 [0,1] 上均匀分布；若向左走，掉下时间为 X，若向右走，掉下时间为 1-X。因此单只蚂蚁的掉下时间仍服从 [0,1] 上的均匀分布。

所以所有蚂蚁全部掉下所需时间是 500 个独立 Uniform(0,1) 随机变量的最大值。若最大值为 M，则 P(M <= x)=x^500，因此 E[M]=500/501。期望时间为 500/501 分钟。

problems.promptEn: Player M has $1 and player N has \$2. Each game gives the winner \$1 from the other. As a better player, M wins 2/3 of the games. They play until one of them is bankrupt. What is the probability that M wins?
problems.promptZh: 玩家 M 有 \$1 and player N has \$2。每场比赛获胜者都会从对方获得 1 美元。作为更好的玩家，M 赢得了 2/3 的游戏。他们一直玩到其中一个破产为止。 M 获胜的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: The most difficult part of Markov chain problems often lies in how to choose the right state space and define the transition probabilities Py 's, Vi, j. This problem has fairly straightforward states. You can define the state space as the combination of the money that player M has ($m) and the money that player N has ($n): \{(m,n)\} = \{(3,0),(2,1),(1,2),(0,3)\}. (Neither m nor n can be negative since the whole game stops when one of them goes bankrupt.) Since the sum of the dollars of both players is always \$3, we can actually simplify the state space using only m: {m} = {0,1,2,3}. The transition graph and the corresponding transition matrix are shown in Figure 5.2. problems.promptEn: Two players bet on roll(s) of the total of two standard six-face dice. Player A bets that a sum of 12 will occur first. Player B bets that two consecutive 7s will occur first. The players keep rolling the dice and record the sums until one player wins. What is the probability that A will win?
problems.promptZh: 两名玩家对总共两个标准六面骰子的掷骰进行投注。玩家 A 下注总和为 12 将首先出现。玩家 B 押注两个连续的 7 将首先出现。玩家不断掷骰子并记录总和，直到一名玩家获胜。 A 获胜的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: Many of the simple Markov chain problems can be solved using pure conditional probability argument. It is not surprising considering that Markov chain is defined as conditional probability: P\{X 1 = 1\cdot  1 X = i X 1 = i 1 • • • X0 = i0 \} = p .. = P\{X 1 = 1\cdot  1 X = i\} n+ n ' n- n- ' ' y n+ n \cdot  So let's first solve the problem using conditional probability arguments. Let P(A) be the probability that A wins. Conditioning P(A) on the first throw's sum F, which has three possible outcomes F = 12, F = 7 and F \{7, 12\}, we have P(A) = P(A IF= 12)P(F = 12) + P(A IF= 7)P(F = 7)+ P(A \{7,12\})P(F \{7, 12\}) Then we tackle each component on the right hand side. Using simple permutation, we can easily see that P(F=12)=1136, P(F=7)=6136, Also it is obvious that P(AIF=12)=1 and (The game essentially starts over again.) To calculate P(A IF= 7), we need to further condition on the second throw's total, which again has three possible outcomes: E = 12, E = 7, and E \{7,12\}. P(A IF= 7) = P(A IF= 7,E = 12)P(E = 12 IF= 7)+P(A IF= 7,E = 7)P(E = 71F=7) + P(A IF= 7, E \{7,12\} )P(E \{7, 12\} IF= 7) = P(A IF= 7,E = 12)x1/36+ P(A IF =7,E = 7)x6/36 = 1x1I36+0x 6136 + P(A)x 29 I 36 = l /36+ 29 I 36P(A) Here the second equation relies on the independence between the second and the first rolls. If F = 7 and E = 12, A wins; if F = 7 and E = 7, A loses; if F = 7 and E !l \{7,12\}, the game essentially starts over again. Now we have all the necessarily information for P( A) . Plugging it into the original equation, we have P(A) = P(A IF= I 2)P(F = 12) + P(A IF= 7)P(F = 7) + P(A IF !l \{7, 12\} )P(F !l \{7, 12\}) =Ix 1/36 + 6136x (1/36 + 29 /36P(A)) + 29 /36P(A) Solving the equation, we get P( A) = 7I13 . This approach, although logically solid, is not intuitively appealing. Now let's try a Markov chain approach. Again the key part is to choose the right state space and define the transition probabilities. It is apparent that we have two absorbing states, 12 (A wins) and 7-7 (B wins), at least two transient states, S (starting state) and 7 (one 7 occurs, yet no 12 or 7-7 occurred). Do we need any other states? Theoretically, you can have other states. In fact, you can use all combination of the outcomes of one roll and two consecutive rolls as states to construct a transition matrix and you will get the same final result. Nevertheless, we want to consolidate as many equivalent states as possible. As we just discussed in the conditional probability approach, if no 12 has occurred and the most recent roll did not yield 7, we essentially go back to the initial starting state S. So all we need are states S, 7, 7-7 and 12. The transition graph and probability to reach state 12 are shown in Figure 5.3. 6/36 cfil)1 Probability to absorption state 12 a12 =I, a1-1 = 0 \} as= l/36 x I +6/36 x a7 + 29/36x as 7 /13 a7 =l / 36xl+6/36x0+29/36xas Figure 5.3 Transition graph and probability to absorption for dice rolls Here the transition probability is again derived from conditional probability arguments. Yet the transition graph makes the process crystal clear.

problems.explanationZh: 许多简单的马尔可夫链问题可以使用纯条件概率论证来解决。考虑到马尔可夫链被定义为条件概率，这并不奇怪： P\{X 1 = 1\cdot 1 X = i X 1 = i 1 • • • X0 = i0 \} = p .. = P\{X 1 = 1\cdot 1 X = i\} n+ n ' n- n- ' ' y n+ n \cdot 所以我们首先使用条件概率论证。令 P(A) 为 A 获胜的概率。将 P(A) 放在第一个投掷的总和 F 上，它具有三种可能的结果 F = 12、F = 7 和 F \{7, 12\}，我们有 P(A) = P(A IF= 12)P(F = 12) + P(A IF= 7)P(F = 7)+ P(A \{7,12\})P(F \{7, 12\}) 然后我们处理右手边。使用简单的排列，我们可以很容易地看出 P(F=12)=1136，P(F=7)=6136，而且很明显 P(AIF=12)=1 并且（游戏本质上又重新开始。）为了计算 P(A IF= 7)，我们需要进一步对第二次投掷的总数进行条件判断，这又具有三种可能的结果：E = 12、E = 7 和 E \{7,12\}。 P(A IF= 7) = P(A IF= 7,E = 12)P(E = 12 IF= 7)+P(A IF= 7,E = 7)P(E = 71F=7) + P(A IF= 7, E \{7,12\} )P(E \{7, 12\} IF= 7) = P(A IF= 7,E = 12)x1/36+ P(A IF =7,E = 7)x6/36 = 1x1I36+0x 6136 + P(A)x 29 I 36 = l /36+ 29 I 36P(A) 这里第二个方程依赖于第二卷和第一卷之间的独立性。如果F = 7且E = 12，则A获胜；如果 F = 7 且 E = 7，则 A 输；如果 F = 7 且 E !l \{7,12\}，则游戏本质上会重新开始。现在我们有了 P( A) 的所有必要信息。将其代入原方程，可得 P(A) = P(A IF= I 2)P(F = 12) + P(A IF= 7)P(F = 7) + P(A IF !l \{7, 12\} )P(F !l \{7, 12\}) =Ix 1/36 + 6136x (1/36 + 29 /36P(A)) + 29 /36P(A) 求解方程，我们得到 P( A) = 7I13 。这种方法虽然逻辑上可靠，但直观上并不吸引人。现在让我们尝试马尔可夫链方法。同样，关键部分是选择正确的状态空间并定义转移概率。很明显，我们有两个吸收状态，12（A 获胜）和 7-7（B 获胜），至少两个瞬态，S（起始状态）和 7（出现一个 7，但没有出现 12 或 7-7）。我们还需要其他国家吗？理论上，你可以有其他状态。事实上，你可以使用一次投掷和连续两次投掷的结果的所有组合作为状态来构造一个转移矩阵，你将得到相同的最终结果。尽管如此，我们希望合并尽可能多的等效状态。正如我们刚刚在条件概率方法中讨论的那样，如果没有发生 12 并且最近的滚动没有产生 7，我们基本上会回到初始状态 S。因此我们需要的只是状态 S、7、7-7 和 12。转换图和到达状态 12 的概率如图 5.3 所示。 6/36 cfil)1 吸收状态的概率 12 a12 =I, a1-1 = 0 \} as= l/36 x I +6/36 x a7 + 29/36x as 7 /13 a7 =l / 36xl+6/36x0+29/36xas 图 5.3 掷骰子的转移图和吸收概率 这里，转移概率再次从条件概率导出论据。然而，转换图使整个过程变得一目了然。

problems.promptEn: Part A. If you keep on tossing a fair coin, what is the expected number of tosses such that you can have HHH (heads heads heads) in a row? What is the expected number of tosses to have THH (tails heads heads) in a row?
problems.promptZh: A 部分：如果你继续抛一枚公平的硬币，那么你需要抛多少次才能连续出现 HHH（正面朝上）？连续出现 THH（反面朝上）的预期抛掷次数是多少？
problems.explanation: The most difficult part of Markov chain is, again, to choose the right state
problems.explanationEn: The most difficult part of Markov chain is, again, to choose the right state
problems.explanationZh: 马尔可夫链最困难的部分还是选择正确的状态

problems.titleEn: Part B. Keep flipping a fair coin until either HHH or THH occurs in the sequence. What is the probability that you get an HHH subsequence before THH?
problems.titleZh: B 部分：继续掷一枚均匀的硬币，直到序列中出现 HHH 或 THH。在 THH 之前得到 HHH 子序列的概率是多少？
tags: Part B. Keep flipping a fair coin until either HHH or THH occurs in the sequence. What is the probability that you get an HHH subsequence before THH?
problems.promptEn: Part B. Keep flipping a fair coin until either HHH or THH occurs in the sequence. What is the probability that you get an HHH subsequence before THH?

problems.titleEn: Part C. (Difficult) Let's add more fun to the triplet game. Instead of fixed triplets for
problems.titleZh: C 部分（难）让我们为三连音游戏添加更多乐趣。而不是固定三元组
tags: Part C. (Difficult) Let's add more fun to the triplet game. Instead of fixed triplets for
problems.promptEn: Part C. (Difficult) Let's add more fun to the triplet game. Instead of fixed triplets for the two players, the new game allows both to choose their own triplets. Player 1 chooses a triplet first and announces it; then player 2 chooses a different triplet. The players again toss the coins until one of the two triplet sequences appears. The player whose chosen triplet appears first wins the game. If both player 1 and player 2 are perfectly rational and both want to maximize their probability of winning, would you go first (as player 1 )? If you go second, what is your probability of winning?
problems.promptZh: C 部分（难）让我们为三连音游戏添加更多乐趣。新游戏不再为两名玩家提供固定的三胞胎，而是允许双方选择自己的三胞胎。玩家1首先选择一个三元组并宣布；然后玩家 2 选择一个不同的三元组。玩家再次抛硬币，直到出现两个三连音序列之一。最先出现的所选择的三元组的玩家赢得游戏。如果玩家 1 和玩家 2 都是完全理性的，并且都希望最大化获胜的概率，那么您会先走（作为玩家 1 ）吗？如果你第二，你获胜的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: A common misconception is that there is always a best sequence that beats other sequences. This misconception is often founded on a wrong assumption that these sequences are transitive: if sequence A has a higher probability occurring before sequence B and sequence B has a higher probability occurring before sequence C, then sequence A has a higher probability occurring before sequence C. In reality, such transitivity does not exist for this game. No matter what sequence player 1 chooses, player 2 can always choose another sequence with more than l/2 probability of winning. The key, as we have indicated in Part B, is to choose the last two coins of the sequence as the first two coins of player l's sequence. We can compile the following table for each pair of sequences: 2's winning Plaver 1 Probability HHH THH HTH HHT TTH THT HTT TTT HHH I l/8/2 3/10 5/12 THH 7/8 v 1/2 3/4/3 1/2/2 HTH l/2/3 3/8/2/2 7/12 M "" HHT 1/2 1/4 2/3 1/2 5/8 2/3 7110 ;;... = TTH 7/10 2/3 5/8/2 v 2/3/4 =:: TUT 7/12 1/2 3/8 1/3 I l/2 HTT l/2 1/2/3 3/4 1/2 7/8 TTT 1/2 5112 3/10 l/2 1/8 I Table 5.1 Player 2's winning probability with different coin sequence pairs As shown in Table 5.1 (you can confirm the results yourself), no matter what player l's choices are, player 2 can always choose a sequence to have better odds of winning. The best sequences that player 2 can choose in response to 1 's choices are highlighted in bold. In order to maximize his odds of winning, player I should choose among HTH, HTT, THH and THT. Even in these cases, player 2 has 2/3 probability of winning.

problems.explanationZh: 一个常见的误解是总有一个优于其他序列的最佳序列。这种误解通常建立在错误的假设之上，即这些序列具有传递性：如果序列 A 在序列 B 之前出现的概率较高，并且序列 B 在序列 C 之前出现的概率较高，那么序列 A 在序列 C 之前出现的概率较高。实际上，该游戏不存在这种传递性。无论玩家 1 选择什么序列，玩家 2 总是可以选择另一个获胜概率大于 l/2 的序列。正如我们在 B 部分中指出的，关键是选择序列的最后两个硬币作为玩家 l 序列的前两个硬币。我们可以为每对序列编制下表： 2 的获胜 Plaver 1 概率 HHH THH HTH HHT TTH THT HTT TTT HHH I l/8/2 3/10 5/12 THH 7/8 v 1/2 3/4/3 1/2/2 HTH l/2/3 3/8/2/2 7/12 M "" HHT 1/2 1/4 2/3 1/2 5/8 2/3 7110 ;;... = TTH 7/10 2/3 5/8/2 v 2/3/4 =:: TUT 7/12 1/2 3/8 1/3 I l/2 HTT l/2 1/2/3 3/4 1/2 7/8 TTT 1/2 5112 3/10 l/2 1/8 I 表 5.1 玩家 2 在不同硬币序列对下的获胜概率 如表 5.1 所示（您可以自己确认结果），无论玩家 l 的选择是什么，玩家 2 总能选择一个序列来获得更好的获胜几率。玩家 2 可以根据 1 的选择选择的最佳序列以粗体突出显示。为了最大化他获胜的几率，玩家 I 应该在 HTH、HTT、THH 和 THT 中进行选择。即使在这些情况下，玩家 2 也有 2/3 的获胜概率。

problems.promptEn: A box contains n balls of n different colors. Each time, you randomly select a pair of balls, repaint the first to match the second, and put the pair back into the box. What is the expected number of steps until all balls in the box are of the same color? (Very difficult)
problems.promptZh: 一个盒子里有n个不同颜色的球。每次，您随机选择一对球，重新绘制第一个球以匹配第二个球，然后将这对球放回盒子中。直到盒子里的所有球都变成相同颜色之前，预计需要走多少步？ （非常困难）
problems.explanation: Let Nn be the number of steps needed to make all balls the same color, and let F;, i = 1, 2, \cdot  \cdot  \cdot , n, be the event that all balls have color i in the end. Applying the law of total expectation, we have Since all the colors are symmetric (i.e., they should have equivalent properties), we have P[F;] = PIF2J = ... = P[Fn] = l In and E[Nn] = E[Nn IF;]= E[Nn I F2] = E[Nn I Fn]. That means we can assume that all the balls have color l in the end and use E[Nn IF;] to represent E[Nn]. So how do we calculate E[Nn IF;]? Not surprisingly, use a Markov chain. Since we only consider event F;, color l is different from other colors and colors 2, \cdot  \cdot  \cdot , n become equivalent. In other words, any pairs of balls that have no color l ball involved are equivalent and any pairs with a color l ball and a ball of another color are equivalent if the order is the same as well. So we only need to use the number of balls that have color l as the states. Figure 5.5 shows the transition graph. Figure 5.5 Transition graph for all n balls to become color 1 Staten is the only absorbing state. Notice that there is no state 0, otherwise it will never reach F; . In fact, all the transition probability is conditioned on F; as well, which makes the transition probability P;,;+i I F; higher than the unconditional probability P;,;+i and P;,;-i IF; lower than P;,;-i \cdot  For example, Pi,o IF; = 0 and Pi,o = 1/ n. (Without conditioning, each ball is likely to be the second ball, so color l has l In probability of being the second ball.) Using the conditional transition probability, the problem essentially becomes expected time to absorption with system equations: E[N; IF;]= l + E[N;-1 IF;] x I';,;-1 IF; +E[N; I F;]x P;,; IF;+ E[N;+1 I F;]x I';,;+1 IF;\cdot  To calculate P;,;-i IF;, let's rewrite the probability as P(xk+i = i -11 xk = i, F; ), V k = 0, 1, .. ,, to make the derivation step clearer: P( . What is the probability of ending in a given color, labeled as c, if i of the balls are of color c? It is simply i In. To see that, we can label the color of each of the i balls of color c as cJ, j = 1, .. \cdot ,i (even though they are in fact the same color). Now it's obvious that all balls will end with color cJ with probability 1 In. The probability for c is the sum of probabilities of c/s, which gives the result i In. Similarly we have P(F; I xk+I = i-1) = (i-1)/ n. For P(xk+I = i-1 I xk = i), we use a basic counting method. There are n(n -1) possible permutations to choose 2 balls out of n balls. problems.explanationZh: 令 Nn 为使所有球颜色相同所需的步数，并令 F;, i = 1, 2, \cdot \cdot \cdot , n, 为所有球最终颜色为 i 的事件。应用总期望定律，我们有 由于所有颜色都是对称的（即它们应该具有相同的属性），所以我们有 P[F;] = PIF2J = ... = P[Fn] = l In 和 E[Nn] = E[Nn IF;]= E[Nn I F2] = E[Nn I Fn]。这意味着我们可以假设所有的球最后都有颜色 l 并使用 E[Nn IF;] 来表示 E[Nn]。那么我们如何计算E[Nn IF;]呢？毫不奇怪，使用马尔可夫链。由于我们只考虑事件F；，颜色l与其他颜色和颜色2不同，\cdot \cdot \cdot ，n变得等价。换句话说，任何不包含颜色 l 球的球对都是等价的，并且如果顺序也相同，则任何包含颜色 l 球和另一种颜色球的球对都是等价的。所以我们只需要使用颜色为 l 的球的数量作为状态。图 5.5 显示了转换图。图 5.5 所有 n 个球变为颜色的转变图 1 Staten 是唯一的吸收态。注意没有状态0，否则永远不会到达F； 。事实上，所有的转移概率都是以F为条件的；同样，这使得转移概率 P;,;+i I F;高于无条件概率 P;,;+i 和 P;,;-i IF;低于 P;,;-i \cdot 例如，Pi,o IF; = 0 且 Pi,o = 1/n。 （在没有条件的情况下，每个球很可能是第二个球，因此颜色 l 成为第二个球的概率为 l。）使用条件转移概率，问题本质上变成系统方程的预期吸收时间：E[N; IF;]= l + E[N;-1 IF;] x I';,;-1 IF; +E[N; I F;]x P;,; IF;+ E[N;+1 I F;]x I';,;+1 IF;\cdot 为了计算 P;,;-i IF;，我们将概率重写为 P(xk+i = i -11 xk = i, F; ), V k = 0, 1, .. ,, 以使推导步骤更清晰： P( . II 'F) P(xk=i,xk+1 =i-1,F;) x =1- x =l k+1 k ' i P(xk = i,F;) P(F; lxk+I =i-1,xk =i)xP(xk+I =i-llxk =i)xP(xk =i) P(F; I xk = i) x P(xk = i) _ P(F; lxk+I =i-l)xP(xk+I =i-llxk =i) P(F; I xk = i) i-1 i(n-i) - x---- = n n(n-l)_(n-i)x(i-1) i/n n(n-1) 第一个方程只是条件概率的定义；第二个方程是贝叶斯定理的应用；第三个方程应用马尔可夫性质。为了导出 P(F; I xk = i) = i 中，我们再次需要使用对称性。我们已经证明，如果所有球都有不同的颜色，那么我们有 P[ F;] = P[ F2 ] = .. \cdot = P[ Fn] = I/ n。如果 i 个球的颜色为 c，则以给定颜色（标记为 c）结束的概率是多少？这只是我在。为了看到这一点，我们可以将每个颜色为 c 的 i 个球的颜色标记为 cJ, j = 1, .. \cdot ,i （即使它们实际上是相同的颜色）。现在很明显，所有球都将以颜色 cJ 结束，概率为 1 In。 c 的概率是 c/s 的概率之和，得出结果 i In。类似地，我们有 P(F; I xk+I = i-1) = (i-1)/ n。对于 P(xk+I = i-1 I xk = i)，我们使用基本计数方法。从 n 个球中选择 2 个球有 n(n -1) 种可能的排列。为了让一个颜色1的球改变颜色，第二个球必须是颜色1，有i个选择；第一个球需要是另一种颜色，有 (n-i) 个选择。 SP( . l I .) i(n -i) o xk+I = z - xk = z = . n(n -1) 应用同样的原理，我们可以得到 。 。 (n-i)x2i 。 。 (n-i)x(i+l) P(xk+i =1lxk =1,F;)= , P(xk+i =1+llxk =1,F;)= 。 n(n-1) n(n-1) 插入 E[ N; I F;] 并简化 E[ N; I F;] 作为 Z;，我们有 (n-i) x 2ix Z; = n(n-1) + (n-i)(i + l)Z;+i + (n-i)(i- l)Z;_1• 利用这些递归系统方程和边界条件 Zn= 0，我们可以得到 Z1=(n-l)2.4

problems.promptEn: A drunk man is at the 17th meter of a 100-meter-long bridge. He has a 50% probability of staggering forward or backward one meter each step. What is the probability that he will make it to the end of the bridge (the lOOth meter) before the beginning (the 0th meter)? What is the expected number of steps he takes to reach either the beginning or the end of the bridge?
problems.promptZh: 一名醉酒男子站在百米长桥的第17米处。他有 50% 的概率每一步向前或向后踉踉跄跄一米。他在桥起点（第 0 米）之前到达桥终点（第 100 米）的概率是多少？他到达桥的起点或终点所需的步数是多少？
problems.explanation: The probability part of the problem-often appearing in different disguises-is among the most popular martingale problems asked by quantitative interviewers. Interestingly, few people use a clear-cut martingale argument. Most candidates either use Markov chain with two absorbing states or treat it as a special version of the gambler's ruin problem with p = 1I2. These approaches yield the correct results in the end, yet a martingale argument is not only simpler but also illustrates the insight behind the problem. Let's set the current pos1t10n (the 17th meter) to O; then the problem becomes a symmetric random walk that stops at either 83 or -17. We also know that both Sn and - n are martingales. Since a martingale stopped at a stopping time is a martingale, s N and - N (where s N = xi + x 2 + ... + x N with N being the stopping time) are martingales as well. Let pa be the probability that it stops at a = 83, p /3 be the probability it stops at -/3 = -17 ( p /3 = 1- pa), and N be the stopping time. Then we have E[ SN] = pa X 83- (1- pa) X 17 = S0 = 0 \} \{ p = 0.17 -N] = E[pa x 832 +(1- Pa) x 172]-E[N] = sg -0 = 0 => = 1441 Hence, the probability that he will make it to the end of the bridge (the 1 OOth meter) before reaching the beginning is 0.17, and the expected number of steps he takes to reach either the beginning or the end of the bridge is 1441. We can easily extend the solution to a general case: a symmetric random walk starting from 0 that stops at either a (a > 0) or -/3 ( p > 0 ). The probability that it stops at a instead of -/3 is Pa = f3 /(a+ jJ). The expected stopping time to reach either a or -/3 is E[ N] = ajJ.
