problems.promptEn: What's the difference between futures and forwards? If the price of the underlying asset is strongly positively correlated with interest rates, and the interest rates are stochastic, which one has higher price: futures or forwards? Why?
problems.promptZh: 期货和远期有什么区别？如果标的资产的价格与利率呈强正相关，并且利率是随机的，那么期货和远期哪一种价格更高？为什么？
problems.explanation: Futures contracts are exchange-traded standardized contracts; forward contracts are over-the-counter agreements so they are more flexible. Futures contracts are marked-to-market daily; forwards contacts are settled at the end of the contract term. If the interest rate is deterministic, futures and forwards have the same theoretical price: F = se<r+u-y)r, where u represents all the storage costs and y represents dividend yield for investment assets, convenience yield for commodities and foreign risk-free interest rate for foreign currencies. The mark-to-market property of futures makes their values differ from forwards when interest rates vary unpredictably (as they do in the real world). As the life of a futures contract increases, the differences between forward and futures contracts may become significant. If the futures price is positively correlated with the interest rate, the increases of the futures price tend to occur the same time when interest rate is high. Because of the mark-to-market feature, the investor who longs the futures has an immediate profit that can be reinvested at a higher rate. The loss tends to occur when the interest rate is low so that it can be financed at a low rate. So a futures contract is more valuable than the forward when its value is positively correlated with interest rates and the futures price should be higher.
problems.explanationEn: Futures contracts are exchange-traded standardized contracts; forward contracts are over-the-counter agreements so they are more flexible. Futures contracts are marked-to-market daily; forwards contacts are settled at the end of the contract term. If the interest rate is deterministic, futures and forwards have the same theoretical price: F = se<r+u-y)r, where u represents all the storage costs and y represents dividend yield for investment assets, convenience yield for commodities and foreign risk-free interest rate for foreign currencies. The mark-to-market property of futures makes their values differ from forwards when interest rates vary unpredictably (as they do in the real world). As the life of a futures contract increases, the differences between forward and futures contracts may become significant. If the futures price is positively correlated with the interest rate, the increases of the futures price tend to occur the same time when interest rate is high. Because of the mark-to-market feature, the investor who longs the futures has an immediate profit that can be reinvested at a higher rate. The loss tends to occur when the interest rate is low so that it can be financed at a low rate. So a futures contract is more valuable than the forward when its value is positively correlated with interest rates and the futures price should be higher.
problems.explanationZh: 期货合约是交易所交易的标准化合约；远期合约是场外交易协议，因此更加灵活。期货合约每日按市价计价；远期合约在合同期结束时结算。如果利率是确定性的，则期货和远期具有相同的理论价格：F = se<r+u-y)r，其中u代表所有存储成本，y代表投资资产的股息收益率、商品的便利收益率和外币的国外无风险利率。当利率变化不可预测时（正如现实世界中的情况一样），期货的按市价计算的特性使其价值不同于远期合约。随着期货合约期限的延长，远期合约和期货合约之间的差异可能会变得很大。如果期货价格与利率呈正相关，则当利率较高时，期货价格往往会同时上涨。由于按市价计价的特点，做多期货的投资者可以立即获得利润，并可以以更高的利率进行再投资。当利率较低时，损失往往会发生，因此可以以较低的利率融资。因此，当期货合约的价值与利率呈正相关时，期货合约比远期合约更有价值，并且期货价格应该更高。

problems.promptEn: Explain some of the basic interest rate models and their differences.
problems.promptZh: 解释一些基本的利率模型及其差异。
problems.answer: Basic interest-rate models include equilibrium short-rate models such as Vasicek and CIR, and no-arbitrage short-rate models such as Ho-Lee and Hull-White; forward-rate models such as HJM model the whole forward curve.
problems.answerEn: Basic interest-rate models include equilibrium short-rate models such as Vasicek and CIR, and no-arbitrage short-rate models such as Ho-Lee and Hull-White; forward-rate models such as HJM model the whole forward curve.
problems.answerZh: 基本利率模型包括 Vasicek、CIR 等均衡短期利率模型，以及 Ho-Lee、Hull-White 等无套利短期利率模型；HJM 等远期利率模型则刻画整条远期曲线。
problems.explanation:
Interest-rate models can be grouped into short-rate models and forward-rate models. Short-rate models describe the instantaneous rate R(t); forward-rate models, such as HJM, describe the whole forward-rate curve.

Equilibrium models do not necessarily match today's term structure. No-arbitrage models are calibrated to the current term structure so that prices are arbitrage-free relative to today's bond market.

Vasicek: dR(t)=kappa(theta-R(t))dt + sigma dW(t). The rate mean-reverts toward theta, but constant volatility allows negative rates.

CIR: dR(t)=kappa(theta-R(t))dt + sigma sqrt(R(t)) dW(t). It is mean-reverting and uses state-dependent volatility; under the usual Feller condition it helps keep rates nonnegative.

Ho-Lee: dr(t)=theta(t)dt + sigma dW(t). The time-dependent drift is chosen to match the current yield curve.

Hull-White: dR(t)=kappa(theta(t)-R(t))dt + sigma dW(t). It extends the Vasicek structure with a time-dependent mean level to fit the current term structure.
problems.explanationEn:
Interest-rate models can be grouped into short-rate models and forward-rate models. Short-rate models describe the instantaneous rate R(t); forward-rate models, such as HJM, describe the whole forward-rate curve.

Equilibrium models do not necessarily match today's term structure. No-arbitrage models are calibrated to the current term structure so that prices are arbitrage-free relative to today's bond market.

Vasicek: dR(t)=kappa(theta-R(t))dt + sigma dW(t). The rate mean-reverts toward theta, but constant volatility allows negative rates.

CIR: dR(t)=kappa(theta-R(t))dt + sigma sqrt(R(t)) dW(t). It is mean-reverting and uses state-dependent volatility; under the usual Feller condition it helps keep rates nonnegative.

Ho-Lee: dr(t)=theta(t)dt + sigma dW(t). The time-dependent drift is chosen to match the current yield curve.

Hull-White: dR(t)=kappa(theta(t)-R(t))dt + sigma dW(t). It extends the Vasicek structure with a time-dependent mean level to fit the current term structure.
problems.explanationZh:
利率模型可以分为短期利率模型和远期利率模型。短期利率模型描述瞬时利率 R(t)，HJM 等远期利率模型描述整条远期利率曲线。

均衡模型不一定匹配当前期限结构；无套利模型会校准到当前期限结构，使价格相对于当前债券市场无套利。

Vasicek: dR(t)=kappa(theta-R(t))dt + sigma dW(t)。利率向长期均值 theta 回复，但常数波动率可能导致负利率。

CIR: dR(t)=kappa(theta-R(t))dt + sigma sqrt(R(t)) dW(t)。它保留均值回复，并使用状态相关波动率；在通常的 Feller 条件下有助于保持利率非负。

Ho-Lee: dr(t)=theta(t)dt + sigma dW(t)。随时间变化的漂移用于匹配当前收益率曲线。

Hull-White: dR(t)=kappa(theta(t)-R(t))dt + sigma dW(t)。它在 Vasicek 结构上加入随时间变化的均值水平，以拟合当前期限结构。

problems.promptEn: How do you swap two integers, i and j, without using additional storage space?
problems.promptZh: 如何在不使用额外存储空间的情况下交换两个整数 i 和 j？
problems.answer:
Use either arithmetic:
$$i=i+j,\quad j=i-j,\quad i=i-j,$$
or XOR:
$$i=i\oplus j,\quad j=i\oplus j,\quad i=i\oplus j.$$
problems.answerEn:
Use either arithmetic:
$$i=i+j,\quad j=i-j,\quad i=i-j,$$
or XOR:
$$i=i\oplus j,\quad j=i\oplus j,\quad i=i\oplus j.$$
problems.answerZh:
可用加减法：
$$i=i+j,\quad j=i-j,\quad i=i-j,$$
也可用异或：
$$i=i\oplus j,\quad j=i\oplus j,\quad i=i\oplus j.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Swap code was garbled and contained wrong symbols.

problems.promptEn: If you are given a sorted array, can you write some code to extract the unique elements from the array? For example, if the array is [1, 1, 3, 3, 3, 5, 5, 5, 9, 9, 9, 9], the unique elements should be [1, 3, 5, 9].
problems.promptZh: 如果给你一个排序数组，你能编写一些代码来从数组中提取唯一元素吗？例如，如果数组为 [1, 1, 3, 3, 3, 5, 5, 5, 9, 9, 9, 9]，则唯一元素应为 [1, 3, 5, 9]。
problems.answer: Scan the sorted array once and append an element only when it differs from the previous element; time O(n), extra output space O(k).
problems.answerEn: Scan the sorted array once and append an element only when it differs from the previous element; time O(n), extra output space O(k).
problems.answerZh: 线性扫描排序数组，只在当前元素不同于前一个元素时加入结果；时间复杂度 O(n)，输出空间 O(k)。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Chinese code comments mistranslated reserve/reallocation.

problems.promptEn:
Write an algorithm to compute
$$y=A_0+A_1x+A_2x^2+\cdots+A_nx^n.$$
problems.promptZh:
写一个算法计算
$$y=A_0+A_1x+A_2x^2+\cdots+A_nx^n.$$
problems.answer:
Use Horner's method:
$$y=A_n; \quad y=yx+A_{n-1};\quad \ldots;\quad y=yx+A_0,$$
which takes $O(n)$ multiplications.
problems.answerEn:
Use Horner's method:
$$y=A_n; \quad y=yx+A_{n-1};\quad \ldots;\quad y=yx+A_0,$$
which takes $O(n)$ multiplications.
problems.answerZh:
使用霍纳算法：
$$y=A_n; \quad y=yx+A_{n-1};\quad \ldots;\quad y=yx+A_0,$$
只需 $O(n)$ 次乘法。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Horner was mistranscribed as Homer and polynomial subscripts were corrupted.

problems.promptEn:
Given a large array $A$ of length $m$, develop an efficient algorithm to compute the $n$-element moving average
$$B_i=\frac{A_{i-n+1}+A_{i-n+2}+\cdots+A_i}{n},\qquad i=n,n+1,\ldots,m.$$
problems.promptZh:
给定长度为 $m$ 的大数组 $A$，设计一个高效算法计算 $n$ 元素移动平均：
$$B_i=\frac{A_{i-n+1}+A_{i-n+2}+\cdots+A_i}{n},\qquad i=n,n+1,\ldots,m.$$
problems.answer: Maintain a rolling sum: initialize the sum of the first n values, then update by subtracting the element leaving the window and adding the new element. Time O(m).
problems.answerEn: Maintain a rolling sum: initialize the sum of the first n values, then update by subtracting the element leaving the window and adding the new element. Time O(m).
problems.answerZh: 维护滚动和：先计算前 n 个元素之和，然后每步减去离开窗口的元素、加上新进入窗口的元素。时间复杂度 O(m)。
problems.explanation:
Compute the first window sum
$$S=A_1+\cdots+A_n,$$
then set $B_n=S/n$. For each $i=n+1,\ldots,m$, update
$$S\leftarrow S-A_{i-n}+A_i,$$
and set
$$B_i=S/n.$$
Each array element is added once and subtracted once, so the algorithm runs in $O(m)$ time.
problems.explanationEn:
Compute the first window sum
$$S=A_1+\cdots+A_n,$$
then set $B_n=S/n$. For each $i=n+1,\ldots,m$, update
$$S\leftarrow S-A_{i-n}+A_i,$$
and set
$$B_i=S/n.$$
Each array element is added once and subtracted once, so the algorithm runs in $O(m)$ time.
problems.explanationZh:
先计算第一个窗口之和
$$S=A_1+\cdots+A_n,$$
并令 $B_n=S/n$。对每个 $i=n+1,\ldots,m$，更新
$$S\leftarrow S-A_{i-n}+A_i,$$
再令
$$B_i=S/n.$$
每个数组元素至多被加一次、减一次，因此算法时间复杂度为 $O(m)$。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Moving-average indexing notation was corrupted.

problems.promptEn: Explain three sorting algorithms for sorting $n$ distinct values $A_1,\ldots,A_n$, and analyze the complexity of each algorithm.
problems.promptZh: 解释三种用于排序 $n$ 个不同值 $A_1,\ldots,A_n$ 的排序算法，并分析各自复杂度。
problems.answer: Insertion sort: average/worst O(n^2). Merge sort: O(n log n). Quicksort: average O(n log n), worst O(n^2).
problems.answerEn: Insertion sort: average/worst O(n^2). Merge sort: O(n log n). Quicksort: average O(n log n), worst O(n^2).
problems.answerZh: 插入排序：平均/最坏 O(n^2)。归并排序：O(n log n)。快速排序：平均 O(n log n)，最坏 O(n^2)。
problems.explanation:
Insertion sort builds a sorted prefix. To insert the $i$th element into the sorted prefix may require $O(i)$ comparisons, so the total average and worst-case time is $O(n^2)$.

Merge sort divides the array into two halves, recursively sorts each half, and merges two sorted halves in linear time. Its recurrence is
$$T(n)=2T(n/2)+O(n),$$
so
$$T(n)=O(n\log n).$$

Quicksort chooses a pivot, partitions the array into elements smaller and larger than the pivot, and recursively sorts both parts. With a random pivot or random input, the average time is $O(n\log n)$. In the worst case, if the pivot repeatedly creates a split of sizes $0$ and $n-1$, the time is $O(n^2)$.
problems.explanationEn:
Insertion sort builds a sorted prefix. To insert the $i$th element into the sorted prefix may require $O(i)$ comparisons, so the total average and worst-case time is $O(n^2)$.

Merge sort divides the array into two halves, recursively sorts each half, and merges two sorted halves in linear time. Its recurrence is
$$T(n)=2T(n/2)+O(n),$$
so
$$T(n)=O(n\log n).$$

Quicksort chooses a pivot, partitions the array into elements smaller and larger than the pivot, and recursively sorts both parts. With a random pivot or random input, the average time is $O(n\log n)$. In the worst case, if the pivot repeatedly creates a split of sizes $0$ and $n-1$, the time is $O(n^2)$.
problems.explanationZh:
插入排序逐步维护一个已排序前缀。把第 $i$ 个元素插入已排序前缀可能需要 $O(i)$ 次比较，所以平均和最坏时间复杂度都是 $O(n^2)$。

归并排序把数组分成两半，递归排序两半，再用线性时间合并两个有序数组。递推式为
$$T(n)=2T(n/2)+O(n),$$
因此
$$T(n)=O(n\log n).$$

快速排序选择一个枢轴，把数组分成小于和大于枢轴的两部分，再递归排序。随机枢轴或随机输入下，平均复杂度为 $O(n\log n)$。最坏情况下，如果每次枢轴都产生 $0$ 和 $n-1$ 的划分，复杂度为 $O(n^2)$。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Sorting complexity notation was corrupted.

problems.promptEn: A. If you have a random number generator that can generate random numbers from either discrete or continuous uniform distributions, how do you shuffle a deck of 52 cards so that every permutation is equally likely?
problems.promptZh: 如果您有一个随机数生成器，可以根据离散或连续均匀分布生成随机数，那么如何洗牌一副 52 张牌，以便每种排列的可能性均等？
problems.explanation:
A simple algorithm to permute $n$ elements is random permutation by sorting. Assign an independent random number to each card and then sort the cards by those random numbers. By symmetry, every one of the $n!$ possible ordered sequences is equally likely. The complexity is determined by sorting, so the running time is $\Theta(n\log n)$. For a small deck with $n=52$, this is acceptable.

For large $n$, a faster approach is the Knuth shuffle, also called the Fisher-Yates shuffle. For elements $A[1], \ldots, A[n]$, loop over $i=1,\ldots,n$ and swap $A[i]$ with $A[\operatorname{Random}(i,n)]$, where $\operatorname{Random}(i,n)$ is drawn uniformly from the integers $i,\ldots,n$. The Knuth shuffle has complexity $\Theta(n)$. At the first step every card has probability $1/n$ of becoming the first card; at the second step every remaining card has probability $1/(n-1)$ of becoming the second card; and so on. Thus every ordered sequence has probability $1/n!$.
problems.explanationEn:
A simple algorithm to permute $n$ elements is random permutation by sorting. Assign an independent random number to each card and then sort the cards by those random numbers. By symmetry, every one of the $n!$ possible ordered sequences is equally likely. The complexity is determined by sorting, so the running time is $\Theta(n\log n)$. For a small deck with $n=52$, this is acceptable.

For large $n$, a faster approach is the Knuth shuffle, also called the Fisher-Yates shuffle. For elements $A[1], \ldots, A[n]$, loop over $i=1,\ldots,n$ and swap $A[i]$ with $A[\operatorname{Random}(i,n)]$, where $\operatorname{Random}(i,n)$ is drawn uniformly from the integers $i,\ldots,n$. The Knuth shuffle has complexity $\Theta(n)$. At the first step every card has probability $1/n$ of becoming the first card; at the second step every remaining card has probability $1/(n-1)$ of becoming the second card; and so on. Thus every ordered sequence has probability $1/n!$.
problems.explanationZh:
排列 $n$ 个元素的一种简单方法是“随机赋值后排序”：给每张牌分配一个独立随机数，然后按随机数排序。由对称性可知，$n!$ 种可能排列中每一种出现的概率都相同。该方法的复杂度由排序步骤决定，因此运行时间为 $\Theta(n\log n)$。当 $n=52$ 时，这种方法通常已经足够。

对于更大的 $n$，可以使用更快的 Knuth shuffle，也称 Fisher-Yates shuffle。对元素 $A[1], \ldots, A[n]$，依次令 $i=1,\ldots,n$，并交换 $A[i]$ 与 $A[\operatorname{Random}(i,n)]$，其中 $\operatorname{Random}(i,n)$ 从整数 $i,\ldots,n$ 中均匀抽取。Knuth shuffle 的复杂度为 $\Theta(n)$。第一步每张牌都有 $1/n$ 的概率成为第一张；第二步剩余每张牌都有 $1/(n-1)$ 的概率成为第二张；依此类推。因此每一种有序排列的概率都是 $1/n!$。

problems.titleEn: B. You have a file consisting of characters. The characters in the file can be read sequen
problems.titleZh: B. 你有一个由字符组成的文件。可以顺序读取文件中的字符
tags: B. You have a file consisting of characters. The characters in the file can be read sequen
problems.promptEn: B. You have a file consisting of characters. The characters in the file can be read sequentially, but the length of the file is unknown. How do you pick a character so that every character in the file has equal probability of being chosen?
problems.promptZh: B. 你有一个由字符组成的文件。可以顺序读取文件中的字符，但文件的长度未知。如何选择一个字符以使文件中的每个字符都有相同的被选择概率？
problems.answer: Use reservoir sampling: keep the first character; when reading the k-th character, replace the current choice with probability 1/k. At the end, every character has probability 1/m if the file has m characters.
problems.answerEn: Use reservoir sampling: keep the first character; when reading the k-th character, replace the current choice with probability 1/k. At the end, every character has probability 1/m if the file has m characters.
problems.answerZh: 使用蓄水池抽样：先保留第一个字符；读到第 k 个字符时，以 1/k 的概率用它替换当前选择。若文件共有 m 个字符，最终每个字符被选中的概率都是 1/m。
problems.explanation:
This is reservoir sampling with reservoir size $1$. After reading the first character, keep it. When the $k$th character is read, replace the stored character with probability $1/k$ and keep the old stored character with probability $(k-1)/k$.

By induction, after $k$ characters have been processed, each of them is stored with probability $1/k$. When character $k+1$ arrives, each old character remains stored with probability
$$\frac1k\cdot\frac{k}{k+1}=\frac{1}{k+1},$$
and the new character is stored with probability $1/(k+1)$. Thus the invariant holds through the end of the file.
problems.explanationEn:
This is reservoir sampling with reservoir size $1$. After reading the first character, keep it. When the $k$th character is read, replace the stored character with probability $1/k$ and keep the old stored character with probability $(k-1)/k$.

By induction, after $k$ characters have been processed, each of them is stored with probability $1/k$. When character $k+1$ arrives, each old character remains stored with probability
$$\frac1k\cdot\frac{k}{k+1}=\frac{1}{k+1},$$
and the new character is stored with probability $1/(k+1)$. Thus the invariant holds through the end of the file.
problems.explanationZh:
这是容量为 $1$ 的蓄水池抽样。读到第一个字符后先保留它。读到第 $k$ 个字符时，以 $1/k$ 的概率用它替换当前保存的字符，并以 $(k-1)/k$ 的概率保留旧字符。

用归纳法可知，处理完 $k$ 个字符后，每个字符被保存的概率都是 $1/k$。当第 $k+1$ 个字符到来时，每个旧字符继续被保存的概率为
$$\frac1k\cdot\frac{k}{k+1}=\frac{1}{k+1},$$
而新字符被保存的概率也是 $1/(k+1)$。因此该不变量一直保持到文件结束。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Reservoir sampling probability notation was corrupted.

problems.titleEn: A. Develop an algorithm to find both the minimum and the maximum of n numbers using no mor
problems.titleZh: A. 开发一种算法来查找 n 个数字的最小值和最大值
tags: A. Develop an algorithm to find both the minimum and the maximum of n numbers using no mor
problems.promptEn: A. Develop an algorithm to find both the minimum and the maximum of n numbers using no more than 3n/2 comparisons.
problems.promptZh: A. 开发一种算法，使用不超过 3n/2 次比较来查找 n 个数字的最小值和最大值。
problems.explanation: For an unsorted array of n numbers, it takes n-1 comparisons to identify either the minimum or the maximum of the array. However, it takes at most 3n/2 comparisons to identify both the minimum and the maximum. If we separate the elements to n/2 pairs, compare the elements in each pair and put the smaller one in group A and the larger one in group B. This step takes n I 2 comparisons. Since the minimum of the whole array must be in group A and the maximum must be in group B, we only need to find the minimum in A and the maximum in B, either of which takes n I 2 - 1 comparisons. So the total number of comparisons is at most 3n/2.5
problems.explanationEn: For an unsorted array of n numbers, it takes n-1 comparisons to identify either the minimum or the maximum of the array. However, it takes at most 3n/2 comparisons to identify both the minimum and the maximum. If we separate the elements to n/2 pairs, compare the elements in each pair and put the smaller one in group A and the larger one in group B. This step takes n I 2 comparisons. Since the minimum of the whole array must be in group A and the maximum must be in group B, we only need to find the minimum in A and the maximum in B, either of which takes n I 2 - 1 comparisons. So the total number of comparisons is at most 3n/2.5
problems.explanationZh: 对于 n 个数字的未排序数组，需要进行 n-1 次比较才能确定数组的最小值或最大值。然而，最多需要 3n/2 次比较才能确定最小值和最大值。如果我们将元素分成 n/2 对，比较每对中的元素，将较小的元素放入 A 组，将较大的元素放入 B 组。此步骤需要进行 n I 2 次比较。由于整个数组的最小值一定在A组，最大值一定在B组，所以我们只需要找到A中的最小值和B中的最大值，两者都需要进行n I 2 - 1次比较。所以总比较次数最多为3n/2.5

problems.titleEn: B. You are given an array of numbers. From the beginning of the array to some position, al
problems.titleZh: B. 在未知长度数组中寻找第一个非零元素
tags: B. You are given an array of numbers. From the beginning of the array to some position, al
problems.promptEn: B. You are given an array of numbers. From the beginning of the array to some position, all elements are zero; after that position, all elements are nonzero. If you don't know the size of the array, how do you find the position of the first nonzero element?
problems.promptZh: B. 给你一个数字数组。从数组的开头到某个位置，所有元素都为零；在该位置之后，所有元素均非零。如果不知道数组的大小，如何找到第一个非零元素的位置？
problems.answer: Use exponential search to bracket the first nonzero position, then binary search inside the bracket. Complexity O(log n), where n is the first nonzero position.
problems.answerEn: Use exponential search to bracket the first nonzero position, then binary search inside the bracket. Complexity O(log n), where n is the first nonzero position.
problems.answerZh: 先用指数搜索找到第一个非零元素所在区间，再在该区间内二分搜索。若第一个非零元素位置为 n，复杂度为 O(log n)。
problems.explanation:
Check positions $1,2,4,8,\ldots$ until finding a nonzero element. Suppose the first nonzero among these probes is at position $2^k$. Then the first nonzero element lies between $2^{k-1}+1$ and $2^k$.

Now run binary search in that interval, using the fact that all entries before the target position are zero and all entries from the target position onward are nonzero. The exponential search takes $O(k)$ probes and the binary search takes $O(k)$ probes. Since $2^{k-1}<n\leq2^k$, we have $k=O(\log n)$.
problems.explanationEn:
Check positions $1,2,4,8,\ldots$ until finding a nonzero element. Suppose the first nonzero among these probes is at position $2^k$. Then the first nonzero element lies between $2^{k-1}+1$ and $2^k$.

Now run binary search in that interval, using the fact that all entries before the target position are zero and all entries from the target position onward are nonzero. The exponential search takes $O(k)$ probes and the binary search takes $O(k)$ probes. Since $2^{k-1}<n\leq2^k$, we have $k=O(\log n)$.
problems.explanationZh:
依次检查位置 $1,2,4,8,\ldots$，直到找到一个非零元素。假设这些探测中第一个非零位置是 $2^k$，则真正的第一个非零元素位于 $2^{k-1}+1$ 到 $2^k$ 之间。

然后在这个区间内做二分搜索，利用数组在目标位置之前全为零、从目标位置开始全为非零的性质。指数搜索需要 $O(k)$ 次探测，二分搜索也需要 $O(k)$ 次探测。由于 $2^{k-1}<n\leq2^k$，所以 $k=O(\log n)$。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Exponential search explanation and Chinese prompt were truncated/corrupted.

problems.titleEn: C. You have a square grid of numbers. The numbers in each row increase from left to right.
problems.titleZh: C. 你有一个数字方格。每行中的数字从左到右递增。
tags: C. You have a square grid of numbers. The numbers in each row increase from left to right.
problems.promptEn: C. You have a square grid of numbers. The numbers in each row increase from left to right. The numbers in each column increase from top to bottom. Design an algorithm to find a given number from the grid. What is the complexity of your algorithm?
problems.promptZh: C. 你有一个数字方格。每行中的数字从左到右递增。每列中的数字从上到下递增。设计一个算法从网格中找到给定的数字。你的算法的复杂度是多少？
problems.answer: Start at the top-right corner. If the current value is larger than the target, move left; if it is smaller, move down. Complexity O(n) for an n by n grid.
problems.answerEn: Start at the top-right corner. If the current value is larger than the target, move left; if it is smaller, move down. Complexity O(n) for an n by n grid.
problems.answerZh: 从右上角开始。若当前值大于目标，向左移动；若当前值小于目标，向下移动。对 n by n 方阵，复杂度为 O(n)。
problems.explanation:
Start at the top-right cell $(1,n)$. At any step:

- If the current value equals the target, stop.
- If the current value is greater than the target, every value below it in the same column is even larger, so eliminate that column and move left.
- If the current value is less than the target, every value to its left in the same row is even smaller, so eliminate that row and move down.

Each step removes one row or one column. In an $n\times n$ grid, there are at most $2n-1$ steps, so the complexity is $O(n)$.
problems.explanationEn:
Start at the top-right cell $(1,n)$. At any step:

- If the current value equals the target, stop.
- If the current value is greater than the target, every value below it in the same column is even larger, so eliminate that column and move left.
- If the current value is less than the target, every value to its left in the same row is even smaller, so eliminate that row and move down.

Each step removes one row or one column. In an $n\times n$ grid, there are at most $2n-1$ steps, so the complexity is $O(n)$.
problems.explanationZh:
从右上角单元格 $(1,n)$ 开始。每一步：

- 如果当前值等于目标，停止。
- 如果当前值大于目标，则同一列下方的值更大，可排除该列并向左移动。
- 如果当前值小于目标，则同一行左侧的值更小，可排除该行并向下移动。

每一步都会排除一行或一列。对 $n\times n$ 方阵，最多走 $2n-1$ 步，因此复杂度为 $O(n)$。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Sorted-matrix search explanation had garbled indices.

problems.answer: It will take about 162 seconds. The naive recursive algorithm is exponential; use iterative dynamic programming in O(n), or matrix exponentiation in O(log n).
problems.answerEn: It will take about 162 seconds. The naive recursive algorithm is exponential; use iterative dynamic programming in O(n), or matrix exponentiation in O(log n).
problems.answerZh: 大约需要 162 秒。朴素递归算法是指数复杂度；应使用 O(n) 的迭代动态规划，或 O(log n) 的矩阵快速幂。
problems.explanation:
The running time T(n) of the naive recursive program satisfies T(n)=T(n-1)+T(n-2)+O(1), so T(n) grows proportionally to the Fibonacci numbers.

For large n, T(n+1)/T(n) approaches the golden ratio phi=(1+sqrt(5))/2, approximately 1.618. Therefore if T(n)=100 seconds, then T(n+1) is about 161.8 seconds, or 162 seconds to the nearest second.

The recursive algorithm is inefficient because it recomputes the same smaller Fibonacci values many times. An iterative dynamic-programming computation stores the two most recent values and runs in O(n) time and O(1) space. Matrix exponentiation using [[1,1],[1,0]]^n can compute the value in O(log n) matrix multiplications.
problems.explanationEn:
The running time T(n) of the naive recursive program satisfies T(n)=T(n-1)+T(n-2)+O(1), so T(n) grows proportionally to the Fibonacci numbers.

For large n, T(n+1)/T(n) approaches the golden ratio phi=(1+sqrt(5))/2, approximately 1.618. Therefore if T(n)=100 seconds, then T(n+1) is about 161.8 seconds, or 162 seconds to the nearest second.

The recursive algorithm is inefficient because it recomputes the same smaller Fibonacci values many times. An iterative dynamic-programming computation stores the two most recent values and runs in O(n) time and O(1) space. Matrix exponentiation using [[1,1],[1,0]]^n can compute the value in O(log n) matrix multiplications.
problems.explanationZh:
这个朴素递归程序的运行时间 T(n) 满足 T(n)=T(n-1)+T(n-2)+O(1)，因此 T(n) 与 Fibonacci 数列同阶增长。

当 n 很大时，T(n+1)/T(n) 趋近黄金比例 phi=(1+sqrt(5))/2，约为 1.618。因此若 T(n)=100 秒，则 T(n+1) 约为 161.8 秒，四舍五入为 162 秒。

该递归算法效率很低，因为它反复计算相同的较小 Fibonacci 值。可以用迭代动态规划只保存最近两个值，时间复杂度 O(n)、空间复杂度 O(1)。也可以用矩阵 [[1,1],[1,0]] 的快速幂，在 O(log n) 次矩阵乘法内完成计算。

problems.promptEn:
Suppose you have a one-dimensional array $A$ of length $n$ containing both positive and negative numbers. Design an algorithm to find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray
$$A[i],A[i+1],\ldots,A[j].$$
problems.promptZh:
假设有一个长度为 $n$ 的一维数组 $A$，其中同时包含正数和负数。设计一个算法，找出任意连续子数组
$$A[i],A[i+1],\ldots,A[j]$$
的最大和。
problems.answer: Use Kadane's algorithm: update the best subarray ending at each position and the global best. Time O(n), space O(1).
problems.answerEn: Use Kadane's algorithm: update the best subarray ending at each position and the global best. Time O(n), space O(1).
problems.answerZh: 使用 Kadane 算法：逐步更新“以当前位置结尾的最佳子数组”和全局最佳值。时间复杂度 O(n)，空间复杂度 O(1)。
problems.explanation:
Let $bestEndingHere$ be the maximum subarray sum ending at the current position, and let $bestSoFar$ be the best sum seen so far. For each value $x$,
$$bestEndingHere=\max(x,bestEndingHere+x),$$
and
$$bestSoFar=\max(bestSoFar,bestEndingHere).$$
This is Kadane's algorithm. It scans the array once, so the time complexity is $O(n)$ and the extra space is $O(1)$.
problems.explanationEn:
Let $bestEndingHere$ be the maximum subarray sum ending at the current position, and let $bestSoFar$ be the best sum seen so far. For each value $x$,
$$bestEndingHere=\max(x,bestEndingHere+x),$$
and
$$bestSoFar=\max(bestSoFar,bestEndingHere).$$
This is Kadane's algorithm. It scans the array once, so the time complexity is $O(n)$ and the extra space is $O(1)$.
problems.explanationZh:
令 $bestEndingHere$ 表示以当前位置结尾的最大子数组和，令 $bestSoFar$ 表示目前见过的最大子数组和。对每个值 $x$，
$$bestEndingHere=\max(x,bestEndingHere+x),$$
并更新
$$bestSoFar=\max(bestSoFar,bestEndingHere).$$
这就是 Kadane 算法。它只需扫描数组一次，因此时间复杂度为 $O(n)$，额外空间为 $O(1)$。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Maximum-subarray prompt/explanation had corrupted summation notation and no answer.

problems.promptEn: How do you determine whether an integer is a power of 2?
problems.promptZh: 如何判断一个整数是否是2的幂？
problems.answer: For positive integers, check $x>0$ and $(x\ \&\ (x-1))=0$.
problems.answerEn: For positive integers, check $x>0$ and $(x\ \&\ (x-1))=0$.
problems.explanation:
A positive power of two has exactly one bit set in binary. For example, $8=2^3$ is $1000_2$. Subtracting $1$ flips that bit and sets all lower bits, so $7$ is $0111_2$. Therefore a positive power of two shares no set bits with one less than itself:
$$x\ \&\ (x-1)=0.$$
The positivity check is needed because $0$ also satisfies the bit expression in many languages.
problems.explanationEn:
A positive power of two has exactly one bit set in binary. For example, $8=2^3$ is $1000_2$. Subtracting $1$ flips that bit and sets all lower bits, so $7$ is $0111_2$. Therefore a positive power of two shares no set bits with one less than itself:
$$x\ \&\ (x-1)=0.$$
The positivity check is needed because $0$ also satisfies the bit expression in many languages.
problems.explanationZh:
正的 2 的幂在二进制中恰好只有一个 bit 为 $1$。例如 $8=2^3$ 写作 $1000_2$。减去 $1$ 会清掉这个 bit，并把更低位全变为 $1$，所以 $7$ 写作 $0111_2$。因此正的 2 的幂与它减一之后没有共同的置位 bit：
$$x\ \&\ (x-1)=0.$$
还需要检查 $x>0$，因为在许多语言中 $0$ 也会满足这个位运算表达式。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Power-of-two bit notation used I/l instead of 1 and lacked an answer.

problems.promptEn: Give a fast way to multiply an integer by 7 without using the multiplication (*) operator?
problems.promptZh: 提供一种无需使用乘法 (*) 运算符即可快速将整数乘以 7 的方法吗？
problems.explanation: Use a left shift to multiply by 8, then subtract the original value. Since `x << 3` equals `8x`, `(x << 3) - x` equals `7x`.
problems.explanationEn: Use a left shift to multiply by 8, then subtract the original value. Since `x << 3` equals `8x`, `(x << 3) - x` equals `7x`.
problems.explanationZh: 使用左移先乘以 8，再减去原值。由于 `x << 3` 等于 `8x`，所以 `(x << 3) - x` 等于 `7x`。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Multiplication-by-7 explanation ended with erroneous x*7.8 and no answer.

problems.promptEn: You are given a fair coin. Can you design a simple game using the fair coin so that your probability of winning is p, where 0 < p < 1?
problems.promptZh: 给你一枚公平硬币。你能否用这枚硬币设计一个简单游戏，使你获胜的概率为 p，其中 0 < p < 1？
problems.answer: Write p in binary and use coin tosses to generate a uniform binary number U; win iff U < p, comparing bits sequentially.
problems.answerEn: Write p in binary and use coin tosses to generate a uniform binary number U; win iff U < p, comparing bits sequentially.
problems.answerZh: 把 p 写成二进制，并用抛硬币生成一个二进制均匀随机数 U；逐位比较，若 U < p 则赢。
problems.explanation:
Write
$$p=0.p_1p_2p_3\cdots\quad\text{in base 2},\qquad p_i\in\{0,1\}.$$
Now toss the fair coin repeatedly to generate bits
$$U=0.s_1s_2s_3\cdots,$$
where heads may be $1$ and tails $0$. Compare $U$ and $p$ bit by bit. At the first index $i$ where $s_i\neq p_i$:

- if $s_i<p_i$, declare a win;
- if $s_i>p_i$, declare a loss.

This is exactly the event $U<p$. Since the fair coin generates a uniform $U$ on $[0,1]$, the winning probability is $P(U<p)=p$. Dyadic rationals have two binary expansions, but the ambiguous equality case has probability zero; one fixed representation is enough.
problems.explanationEn:
Write
$$p=0.p_1p_2p_3\cdots\quad\text{in base 2},\qquad p_i\in\{0,1\}.$$
Now toss the fair coin repeatedly to generate bits
$$U=0.s_1s_2s_3\cdots,$$
where heads may be $1$ and tails $0$. Compare $U$ and $p$ bit by bit. At the first index $i$ where $s_i\neq p_i$:

- if $s_i<p_i$, declare a win;
- if $s_i>p_i$, declare a loss.

This is exactly the event $U<p$. Since the fair coin generates a uniform $U$ on $[0,1]$, the winning probability is $P(U<p)=p$. Dyadic rationals have two binary expansions, but the ambiguous equality case has probability zero; one fixed representation is enough.
problems.explanationZh:
把
$$p=0.p_1p_2p_3\cdots$$
写成二进制，其中 $p_i\in\{0,1\}$。然后反复抛公平硬币生成二进制位
$$U=0.s_1s_2s_3\cdots,$$
例如正面记为 $1$，反面记为 $0$。逐位比较 $U$ 和 $p$。在第一个满足 $s_i\neq p_i$ 的位置：

- 若 $s_i<p_i$，判定获胜；
- 若 $s_i>p_i$，判定失败。

这正是事件 $U<p$。公平硬币生成的 $U$ 在 $[0,1]$ 上均匀分布，因此获胜概率为 $P(U<p)=p$。二进制有限小数有两种表示，但恰好相等的情况概率为零；固定采用一种表示即可。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Fair-coin simulation had I/1 OCR errors and broken binary notation.

problems.promptEn: You've got 1000 bottles of wines for a birthday party. Twenty hours before the party, the winery sent you an urgent message that one bottle of wine was poisoned. You happen to have IO lab mice that can be used to test whether a bottle of wine is poisonous. The poison is so strong that any amount will kill a mouse in exactly 18 hours. But before the death on the 18th hour, there are no other symptoms. Is there a sure way that you can find the poisoned bottle using the 10 mice before the party?
problems.promptZh: 您为生日聚会准备了 1000 瓶葡萄酒。聚会前二十个小时，酒庄给你发来紧急消息，一瓶酒中毒了。你碰巧有 IO 实验室老鼠，可以用来测试一瓶酒是否有毒。这种毒药的毒性很强，任何剂量都会在 18 小时内杀死一只老鼠。但在18小时死亡前，没有其他症状。有没有办法在聚会前用 10 只老鼠找到有毒的瓶子？
problems.explanation: If the mice can be tested sequentially to eliminate half of the bottles each time, the problem becomes a simple binary search problem. Ten mice can identify the poisonous bottle in up to 1024 bottles of wines. Unfortunately, since the symptom won't show up until 18 hours later and we only have 20 hours, we cannot sequentially test the mice. Nevertheless, the binary search idea still applies. All integers between 1 and 1000 can be expressed in 10-bit binary format. For example, bottle 1000 can be labeled as 1111101000 since 1000 = 2 9 + 2 8 + 2 7 + 2 6 + 2 5 + 2 3 . Now let mouse I take a sip from every bottle that has a I in the first bit (the lowest bit on the right); let mouse 2 take a sip from every bottle with a 1 in the second bit; ... ;and, finally, let mouse 10 take a sip from every bottle with a 1 in the 10th bit (the highest bit). Eighteen hours later, if we line up the mice from the highest to the lowest bit and treat a live mouse as 0 and a dead mouse as I, we can easily back track the label of the poisonous bottle. For example, if the 6th, 7th, and 9th mice are dead and all others are alive, the line-up gives the sequence 0I01100000 and the label for the poisonous bottle is 28 + 2 6 + 25 =352.
problems.explanationEn: If the mice can be tested sequentially to eliminate half of the bottles each time, the problem becomes a simple binary search problem. Ten mice can identify the poisonous bottle in up to 1024 bottles of wines. Unfortunately, since the symptom won't show up until 18 hours later and we only have 20 hours, we cannot sequentially test the mice. Nevertheless, the binary search idea still applies. All integers between 1 and 1000 can be expressed in 10-bit binary format. For example, bottle 1000 can be labeled as 1111101000 since 1000 = 2 9 + 2 8 + 2 7 + 2 6 + 2 5 + 2 3 . Now let mouse I take a sip from every bottle that has a I in the first bit (the lowest bit on the right); let mouse 2 take a sip from every bottle with a 1 in the second bit; ... ;and, finally, let mouse 10 take a sip from every bottle with a 1 in the 10th bit (the highest bit). Eighteen hours later, if we line up the mice from the highest to the lowest bit and treat a live mouse as 0 and a dead mouse as I, we can easily back track the label of the poisonous bottle. For example, if the 6th, 7th, and 9th mice are dead and all others are alive, the line-up gives the sequence 0I01100000 and the label for the poisonous bottle is 28 + 2 6 + 25 =352.
problems.explanationZh: 如果可以顺序测试老鼠每次消除一半的瓶子，那么问题就变成了一个简单的二分查找问题。 10只老鼠可以识别多达1024瓶葡萄酒中的有毒瓶子。不幸的是，由于症状要到18小时后才会出现，而我们只有20小时，所以我们无法对小鼠进行连续测试。尽管如此，二分查找的思想仍然适用。 1 到 1000 之间的所有整数都可以用 10 位二进制格式表示。例如，瓶子 1000 可以标记为 1111101000，因为 1000 = 2 9 + 2 8 + 2 7 + 2 6 + 2 5 + 2 3 。现在让老鼠 I 从每个第一位（右侧最低位）有 I 的瓶子中喝一口；让老鼠 2 喝一口第二位数字为 1 的瓶子； ...；最后，让老鼠 10 喝一口第 10 位（最高位）为 1 的瓶子。十八小时后，如果我们将老鼠从最高位到最低位排列，将活老鼠视为0，将死老鼠视为I，我们可以轻松回溯毒瓶的标签。例如，如果第 6、7、9 只老鼠死了，而其他所有老鼠都活着，则排列给出的序列为 0I01100000，毒瓶的标签为 28 + 2 6 + 25 =352。

problems.titleEn: A. Explain how you can use Monte Carlo simulation to price a European call option?
problems.titleZh: A. 解释一下如何使用蒙特卡罗模拟来为欧式看涨期权定价？
tags: A. Explain how you can use Monte Carlo simulation to price a European call option?
problems.promptEn: A. Explain how you can use Monte Carlo simulation to price a European call option?
problems.promptZh: A. 解释一下如何使用蒙特卡罗模拟来为欧式看涨期权定价？
problems.answer:
Simulate risk-neutral terminal prices and discount the sample-average payoff:
$$C_0\approx e^{-rT}\frac1M\sum_{k=1}^M \max(S_T^{(k)}-K,0).$$
problems.answerEn:
Simulate risk-neutral terminal prices and discount the sample-average payoff:
$$C_0\approx e^{-rT}\frac1M\sum_{k=1}^M \max(S_T^{(k)}-K,0).$$
problems.answerZh:
模拟风险中性测度下的到期价格，并贴现样本平均收益：
$$C_0\approx e^{-rT}\frac1M\sum_{k=1}^M \max(S_T^{(k)}-K,0).$$
problems.explanation:
Under the risk-neutral measure, a geometric Brownian stock can be simulated by
$$S_{t_{i+1}}=S_{t_i}\exp\left((r-\tfrac12\sigma^2)\Delta t+\sigma\sqrt{\Delta t}\,Z_i\right),$$
where $Z_i$ are IID standard normal random variables. For a European call, path dependence is absent, so one time step to $T$ is enough:
$$S_T^{(k)}=S_0\exp\left((r-\tfrac12\sigma^2)T+\sigma\sqrt{T}\,Z_k\right).$$
For each simulated path, compute the payoff $\max(S_T^{(k)}-K,0)$. The Monte Carlo estimator is the discounted sample average:
$$C_0\approx e^{-rT}\frac1M\sum_{k=1}^M \max(S_T^{(k)}-K,0).$$
problems.explanationEn:
Under the risk-neutral measure, a geometric Brownian stock can be simulated by
$$S_{t_{i+1}}=S_{t_i}\exp\left((r-\tfrac12\sigma^2)\Delta t+\sigma\sqrt{\Delta t}\,Z_i\right),$$
where $Z_i$ are IID standard normal random variables. For a European call, path dependence is absent, so one time step to $T$ is enough:
$$S_T^{(k)}=S_0\exp\left((r-\tfrac12\sigma^2)T+\sigma\sqrt{T}\,Z_k\right).$$
For each simulated path, compute the payoff $\max(S_T^{(k)}-K,0)$. The Monte Carlo estimator is the discounted sample average:
$$C_0\approx e^{-rT}\frac1M\sum_{k=1}^M \max(S_T^{(k)}-K,0).$$
problems.explanationZh:
在风险中性测度下，几何布朗运动股票价格可用
$$S_{t_{i+1}}=S_{t_i}\exp\left((r-\tfrac12\sigma^2)\Delta t+\sigma\sqrt{\Delta t}\,Z_i\right),$$
其中 $Z_i$ 是独立标准正态随机变量。对欧式看涨期权，由于没有路径依赖，直接模拟到 $T$ 即可：
$$S_T^{(k)}=S_0\exp\left((r-\tfrac12\sigma^2)T+\sigma\sqrt{T}\,Z_k\right).$$
对每条模拟路径计算收益 $\max(S_T^{(k)}-K,0)$。Monte Carlo 估计量为贴现后的样本平均：
$$C_0\approx e^{-rT}\frac1M\sum_{k=1}^M \max(S_T^{(k)}-K,0).$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Monte Carlo option-pricing category and formulas were corrupted.

problems.titleEn: B. Generate normal random variables from uniforms
problems.titleZh: B. 用均匀随机数生成正态随机变量
problems.promptEn: B. If your computer can only generate continuous uniform random variables on $[0,1]$, how can you generate random variables with distribution $N(\mu,\sigma^2)$?
problems.promptZh: B. 如果计算机只能生成 $[0,1]$ 上的连续均匀随机变量，如何生成服从 $N(\mu,\sigma^2)$ 的随机变量？
problems.answer: Generate a standard normal $Z$ from uniforms, for example $Z=\Phi^{-1}(U)$ or via Box-Muller, then return $\mu+\sigma Z$.
problems.answerEn: Generate a standard normal $Z$ from uniforms, for example $Z=\Phi^{-1}(U)$ or via Box-Muller, then return $\mu+\sigma Z$.
problems.answerZh: 先由均匀随机数生成标准正态 $Z$，例如 $Z=\Phi^{-1}(U)$ 或使用 Box-Muller 方法，再返回 $\mu+\sigma Z$。
problems.explanation:
One general method is inverse transform sampling. If $U\sim U(0,1)$ and $\Phi$ is the standard normal CDF, then
$$Z=\Phi^{-1}(U)$$
has the standard normal distribution. Therefore
$$X=\mu+\sigma Z$$
has distribution $N(\mu,\sigma^2)$.

Another common method is Box-Muller. If $U_1,U_2$ are independent uniforms on $(0,1)$, then
$$Z_1=\sqrt{-2\ln U_1}\cos(2\pi U_2),\qquad
Z_2=\sqrt{-2\ln U_1}\sin(2\pi U_2)$$
are independent standard normal random variables. Scaling by $\mu+\sigma Z$ gives the desired normal distribution.
problems.explanationEn:
One general method is inverse transform sampling. If $U\sim U(0,1)$ and $\Phi$ is the standard normal CDF, then
$$Z=\Phi^{-1}(U)$$
has the standard normal distribution. Therefore
$$X=\mu+\sigma Z$$
has distribution $N(\mu,\sigma^2)$.

Another common method is Box-Muller. If $U_1,U_2$ are independent uniforms on $(0,1)$, then
$$Z_1=\sqrt{-2\ln U_1}\cos(2\pi U_2),\qquad
Z_2=\sqrt{-2\ln U_1}\sin(2\pi U_2)$$
are independent standard normal random variables. Scaling by $\mu+\sigma Z$ gives the desired normal distribution.
problems.explanationZh:
一种通用方法是逆变换抽样。若 $U\sim U(0,1)$，且 $\Phi$ 为标准正态分布函数，则
$$Z=\Phi^{-1}(U)$$
服从标准正态分布。因此
$$X=\mu+\sigma Z$$
服从 $N(\mu,\sigma^2)$。

另一种常用方法是 Box-Muller。若 $U_1,U_2$ 是 $(0,1)$ 上独立均匀随机变量，则
$$Z_1=\sqrt{-2\ln U_1}\cos(2\pi U_2),\qquad
Z_2=\sqrt{-2\ln U_1}\sin(2\pi U_2)$$
是两个独立标准正态随机变量。再用 $\mu+\sigma Z$ 缩放，即得到目标正态分布。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Normal-random-variable generation was categorized as coding and had garbled formulas.

problems.titleEn: C. Can you explain a few variance reduction techniques to improve the efficiency of Monte Carlo simulation?
problems.titleZh: C. 您能解释一下一些提高蒙特卡罗模拟效率的方差减少技术吗？
tags: C. Can you explain a few variance reduction techniques to improve the efficiency of Monte Carlo simulation?
problems.promptEn: C. Can you explain a few variance reduction techniques to improve the efficiency of Monte Carlo simulation?
problems.promptZh: C. 您能解释一下一些提高蒙特卡罗模拟效率的方差减少技术吗？
problems.answer: Common variance reduction methods include antithetic variables, moment matching, control variates, and importance sampling.
problems.answerEn: Common variance reduction methods include antithetic variables, moment matching, control variates, and importance sampling.
problems.answerZh: 常见方差缩减方法包括对偶变量、矩匹配、控制变量和重要性抽样。
problems.explanation:
A basic Monte Carlo estimator is the average of IID samples. If each sample has variance sigma^2, the estimator variance is sigma^2/M for M simulations, so reducing the sample variance can greatly improve efficiency.

Antithetic variables pair each random draw with a negatively correlated draw, such as using both Z and -Z for normal shocks. Averaging the paired payoffs reduces variance when the payoffs are negatively correlated.

Moment matching rescales or recenters simulated samples so their sample mean and variance match the target distribution more closely.

Control variates use a related quantity Y with known expectation. If X is the quantity of interest, estimate X with X + beta(E[Y]-Y), choosing beta to reduce variance.

Importance sampling draws from a different density g(x) and weights by f(x)/g(x), so E_f[h(X)] = E_g[h(X)f(X)/g(X)]. A good choice of g samples more often from the region that contributes most to the payoff.
problems.explanationEn:
A basic Monte Carlo estimator is the average of IID samples. If each sample has variance sigma^2, the estimator variance is sigma^2/M for M simulations, so reducing the sample variance can greatly improve efficiency.

Antithetic variables pair each random draw with a negatively correlated draw, such as using both Z and -Z for normal shocks. Averaging the paired payoffs reduces variance when the payoffs are negatively correlated.

Moment matching rescales or recenters simulated samples so their sample mean and variance match the target distribution more closely.

Control variates use a related quantity Y with known expectation. If X is the quantity of interest, estimate X with X + beta(E[Y]-Y), choosing beta to reduce variance.

Importance sampling draws from a different density g(x) and weights by f(x)/g(x), so E_f[h(X)] = E_g[h(X)f(X)/g(X)]. A good choice of g samples more often from the region that contributes most to the payoff.
problems.explanationZh:
基本 Monte Carlo 估计量是独立同分布样本的平均值。若单个样本方差为 sigma^2，M 次模拟后的估计量方差为 sigma^2/M，因此降低单次样本方差能显著提高效率。

对偶变量会把每个随机样本与一个负相关样本配对，例如正态扰动同时使用 Z 和 -Z。当两个 payoff 负相关时，取平均可以降低方差。

矩匹配会对模拟样本重新平移或缩放，使样本均值、方差等矩更接近目标分布。

控制变量使用一个期望已知且与目标量相关的变量 Y。若 X 是目标量，可用 X + beta(E[Y]-Y) 进行估计，并选择 beta 来降低方差。

重要性抽样改从另一个密度 g(x) 抽样，并乘以权重 f(x)/g(x)，即 E_f[h(X)] = E_g[h(X)f(X)/g(X)]。好的 g 会更多抽到对 payoff 贡献最大的区域。

problems.titleEn: B. If you are solving a parabolic partial differential equation using the explicit finite
problems.titleZh: B. 如果您正在使用显式有限方程求解抛物型偏微分方程
tags: B. If you are solving a parabolic partial differential equation using the explicit finite
problems.promptEn: B. If you are solving a parabolic partial differential equation using the explicit finite difference method, is it worse to have too many steps in the time dimension or too many steps in the space dimension?
problems.promptZh: B. 如果使用显式有限差分法求解抛物型偏微分方程，时间维度上的步骤过多还是空间维度上的步骤过多更糟糕？
problems.explanation:
The equation for the explicit finite difference method is u;+' =au;_,+(1-2a)u;+au;+" where a=11t/(/l.x)2• For the explicit finite difference method to be stable, we need to have 1-2a > 0 11t /(!l.x)2 < 1I2. So a small 11t (i.e., many time steps) is desirable, but a small !l.x (too many space steps) may make 11t /(/l.x)2 > 1I2 and the results unstable. In that sense, it is worse to have too many steps in the space dimension. In contrast, the implicit difference method is always stable and convergent.
\end{document}
problems.explanationEn:
The equation for the explicit finite difference method is u;+' =au;_,+(1-2a)u;+au;+" where a=11t/(/l.x)2• For the explicit finite difference method to be stable, we need to have 1-2a > 0 11t /(!l.x)2 < 1I2. So a small 11t (i.e., many time steps) is desirable, but a small !l.x (too many space steps) may make 11t /(/l.x)2 > 1I2 and the results unstable. In that sense, it is worse to have too many steps in the space dimension. In contrast, the implicit difference method is always stable and convergent.
\end{document}
problems.explanationZh:
显式有限差分法的方程为 u;+' =au;_,+(1-2a)u;+au;+" 其中 a=11t/(/l.x)2• 为了使显式有限差分法稳定，我们需要 1-2a > 0 11t /(!l.x)2 < 1I2。因此，较小的 11t（即许多时间步）是可取的，但较小的 !l.x （太多的空间步数）可能会使 11t /(/l.x)2 > 1I2 导致结果不稳定。从这个意义上来说，空间维数的步数越多，效果就越差。相比之下，隐式差分法总是稳定且收敛的。
\end{document}
